[75514-0] Lupus anticoagulant two screening tests W Reflex [interpretation] -- **Component:** Lupus anticoagulant two screening tests W Reflex **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Coag  **Short name:** LA 2 screen W Reflex-Imp  **Definition:** The answers included in the example answer list are based on the 2014 CLSI guidelines. If neither the screening assays suggest the presence of LA, the overall result would be "LA not detected." If both of the assays are suggest the presence of LA, the overall result would be "LA detected." If the presence or absence of LA cannot be confirmed without repeat testing, the overall result is "LA indeterminate." The 2009 ISTH guidelines include an alternative answer list with the following answers : 1. Findings compatible with the absence of lupus anticoagulants; 2. Findings compatible with the presence of lupus anticoagulants; and 3. To be tested again in 1 week.
[7551-5] Orange Tree IgE Ab [Units/volume] in Serum -- **Component:** Citrus sinensis tree Ab.IgE **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Orange Tree IgE Qn
[75515-7] Lupus anticoagulant aPTT and dRVVT screening panel W Reflex -- **Component:** Lupus anticoagulant two screening tests W Reflex panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** - **Method:** Coag  **Short name:** LA aPTT+dRVVT screen W reflex panel  **Definition:** The development of this panel for detecting Lupus Anticoagulant (LA) was shaped by the 2009 International Society of Thrombosis and Haemostasis (ISTH) and 2014 Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) guidelines for lupus anticoagulant testing. They both recommend using two separate coagulation tests and identify dRVVT and aPPT as the two best choices. This aPPT & dRVVT panel is most closely aligned with the ISTH guidelines. The testing cascade starts with the use of the two above-named tests to screen for or verify a clotting abnormality, and if either is abnormal, reflexing to the same test with 1:1 pooled plasma (to exclude clotting factor deficiencies) and again with excess phospholipid (PL). Correction of the coagulopathy with excess phospholipid confirms the likelihood of LA. Neither published guidance nor laboratories agree on the order in which the two kinds of reflex tests should be done, and this panel does not take a position on that subject. Some laboratories report the absolute values of the test and some the normalized ratios. So LOINC includes both as alternatives. Only one or the other of the alternatives must be included in the panel report. A summary interpretation is required by both guidelines.
[75516-5] Cortisol Free [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine -- **Component:** Cortisol.free **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortis F 24h Ur-sCnc
[75517-3] Protein.monoclonal band 4 [Mass/time] in 24 hour Urine by Electrophoresis -- **Component:** Protein.monoclonal band 4 **Property:** MRat **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Electrophoresis  **Short name:** M Protein 4 24h Ur Elph-mRate
[75518-1] Bacteria identified in Cerebral spinal fluid by Latex agglutination -- **Component:** Bacteria **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Nom **Method:** LA  **Short name:** Bacteria CSF LA  **Definition:** A latex agglutination test for antigens in CSF in cases of suspected bacterial meningitis.  The most common bacteria antigens tested for are Haemophilus influenzae; Streptococcus pneumoniae; Neisseria meningitidis A; Neisseria meningitidis B; Neisseria meningitidis C. Because the primary purpose  is the identification of N. meningitidis, S. pneumoniae, and H. influenzae from clinical specimens collected from suspected cases of bacterial meningitis, the methods do  not allow identification of other isolates that may be of clinical importance but are less likely encountered. In the US rarely used due to very poor positive predictive value.  Occasionally used to diagnose bacterial meningitis in a patient who is pre-treated with antibiotics. Given current vaccines available in the US (example: pneumococcal, haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) vaccine), bacterial meningitis is a rare event in the US.

[75533-0] NMMDS accreditation, certification, and licensure panel [NMMDS] -- **Component:** Accreditation, certification, & licensure panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Nursing unit} **Scale:** - **Method:** NMMDS  **Definition:** The set of terms in this panel are used to indicate quality assurance organizations of the nursing delivery unit/service by 3 different quality measure categories: accreditation, certification and licensure. Accreditation is a seal of approval given by private, nationally recognized groups that check on the quality of care at health care facilities and organizations. Health care organizations must meet certain quality standards in order to be accredited. Certification is the formal recognition of the knowledge, skills, and experience demonstrated by the achievement of standards that are identified by the profession² (ANA, 2009). Licensure is the granting of authority to practice² (ANA, 2009). State agencies determine the requirements for licensure and examine the competency necessary to meet quality standards.

[75540-5] Hepatitis A virus RNA+Parvovirus B19 DNA [Interpretation] in Plasma from Donor Qualitative by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Hepatitis A virus RNA+Parvovirus B19 DNA **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas^Donor **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** HAV RNA+B19V DNA Plas Donr NAA+probe-Imp  **Definition:** Simultaneously detects hepatitis A virus (HAV) RNA (genotypes 1-3) and parvovirus B19 (B19) DNA (genotypes 1-3) in human donor plasma, either from whole blood (recovered plasma) or collected by apheresis, prior to further manufacture. The aim is to reduce the B19 burden and remove HAV from plasma pools. This term represents the composite result of the individual HAV qualitative test and B19 quantitative test. If either result is reactive, the composite result is reactive; otherwise, it is nonreactive. Plasma may be tested as individual specimens or in pools. Not intended for use on samples of cord blood or as an aid in diagnosis. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the cobas TaqScreen DPX test kit.

[75547-0] Noninvasive prenatal fetal aneuploidy and microdeletion panel based on Plasma cell-free+WBC DNA by Dosage of chromosome-specific circulating cell free (ccf) DNA -- **Component:** Noninvasive prenatal fetal aneuploidy and microdeletion panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** WBC.DNA+Plas.cfDNA **Scale:** - **Method:** Dosage of chromosome specific cf DNA  **Short name:** NIP aneu microdel pnl WBC.DNA+cfDNA  **Definition:** Noninvasive prenatal screening is used to assess fetal risk for various genetic conditions without requiring invasive maternal procedures such as amniocentesis or chorionic villus sampling. Cell-free DNA (cfDNA) in maternal plasma comes from both fetal and maternal sources, and depending on the technology used, this DNA can be used to determine fetal risk for a variety of conditions including aneuploidy (e.g., Trisomy 21, Monosomy X) and microdeletions (e.g., 22q11.2 deletion). This panel was developed for, but is not limited in use to, Natera's Panorama prenatal test, which incorporates SNP analysis of maternal white blood cell DNA with combined maternal and fetal cfDNA SNP analysis. Natera's proprietary informatics analysis method (NATUS) effectively subtracts the maternal genotype from the cfDNA genotype and analyzes the remaining fetal cfDNA genotype for certain aneuploidies and microdeletion syndromes.

[75609-8] Birth defects Outpatient Registry report -- **Component:** Registry report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Outpatient **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Birth defects  **Short name:** Birth defects OP Registry rpt  **Definition:** Birth defect reporting may include congenital or structural malformation, or a biochemical or genetic disease, or any information relevant to incidents of birth defects. Major document sections for this guide include: 1) Birth defects diagnosis, 2) Coded results (relevant patient diagnosis procedures), 3) Payers section, 4) Cytogenetics section, 5) Labor and delivery history and physical, 6) Labor and delivery events, 7) Newborn delivery, 8) Care plan, and 9) the (patient) Disposition section.

[75619-7] National Health Care Surveys report -- **Component:** National health care surveys report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** NHCS report  **Definition:** The National Health Care Surveys (NHCS) report document contains data collected from physicians, ambulatory care centers, and hospitals about visits to inpatient, outpatient, and emergency department settings. [75622-1] HIV 1 and 2 tests - Meaningful Use set -- **Component:** HIV 1 & 2 tests - Meaningful Use set **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** HIV 1 and 2 - MU Set  **Definition:** Panel for qualitative or quantitative tests typically ordered (or observed) for HIV 1 and/or 2, or HIV virus group (e.g. M, N, O, P), antibody or antigen. The panel was created for Meaningful Use 3 and is used in the following eMeasures: (1) HIV Screening for Patients with Sexually Transmitted Infection (STI) (eMeasure ID: 329) and (2) "HIV Screening (eMeasure ID: 349). The goal of the eMeasures is to make sure a patient is screened for HIV, OR that patient is EXEMPT from screening if previous HIV test is performed that evidences patient's HIV status, regardless how that previous test was done (antibody, antigen, RNA, etc.). This concept may be used by a physician to document that he/she has reviewed the HIV 1 and/or 2 test results performed by an outside lab. When the physician does not know which specific LOINC code to choose when documenting the test and results from the lab report, this more generic LOINC panel code can be used. The LOINC codes included in the panel were selected and reviewed by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
[75624-7] Total score [AUDIT] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** AUDIT  **Definition:** The total score on the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT) ranges from 0 to 40 and reflects the extent of alcohol involvement along a broad continuum of severity. Total scores of 8 or more suggest possible hazardous and harmful alcohol use.(WHO, 2012) Comments from patients about their drinking may provide the interviewer with information that can be valuable in the interpretation of the AUDIT total score.
[75626-2] Total score [AUDIT-C] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** AUDIT-C  **Definition:** The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test C (AUDIT-C) is scored on a scale of 0-12 where the higher the score, the more likely the patient's drinking is hazardous. A score of 4 or more for men and 3 or more for women is considered positive for hazardous drinking or active alcohol use disorders. If the points are all from Question 1 alone where 2 and 3 are 0, it is likely the patient is drinking below recommended limits. The care provider may review the patients alcohol intake over that past few months to confirm accuracy. [PMID: 12695273]

[75633-8] Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score Calculated by VectraDA -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** Calculated.VectraDA  **Short name:** RA DAS Score Calc VectraDA  **Definition:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score is based on the measurements of 12 serum proteins. An algorithm is applied to these concentrations to calculate a quantitative disease activity score ranging from 1 to 100. Test results are intended to aid in the assessment of disease activity in RA patients when used in conjunction with a standard clinical assessment. The test is not intended or validated to diagnose RA. This term is based on Crescendo BioScience's Vectra DA blood test for rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.
[75634-6] Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score level Qualitative by VectraDA -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score level **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord **Method:** VectraDA  **Short name:** RA DAS level Ql VectraDA  **Definition:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score level is defined as low, moderate or high where scores 1 to 29 are "low" activity level, 30-44 are "moderate" activity level, and 45-100 are "high" activity level. The activity level is based on analysis of multiple biomarkers associated with rheumatoid arthritis. This term was created for Crescendo BioScience's Vectra DA blood test for rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.
[75635-3] Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity panel - Serum or Plasma by VectraDA -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** - **Method:** VectraDA  **Short name:** RA Disease Activity Pnl SerPl VectraDA  **Definition:** Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) disease activity panel includes the assessment of multiple biomarkers in a patient's serum to determine overall disease activity in adults diagnosed with RA. Currently, 12 biomarkers are evaluated, including epidermal growth factor (EGF), vascular endothelial growth factor A (VEGF-A), leptin, interleukin 6 (IL-6), serum amyloid A (SAA), CRP, vascular cell adhesion molecule 1 (VCAM-1), matrix metalloproteinase 1 (MMP-1), matrix metalloproteinase 3 (MMP-3), tumor necrosis factor receptor superfamily member 1A (TNF-RI), human cartilage glycoprotein 39 (YKL-40), and resistin. The assessment includes the overall RA disease activity score, score level (high, medium, low), and score confidence interval range (lower and upper limits). This panel term was created for Crescendo BioScience's VectraDA blood test for rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.

[75639-5] Lactate dehydrogenase in body fluid/Lactate dehydrogenase in serum -- **Component:** Lactate dehydrogenase.body fld/Lactate dehydrogenase.serum **Property:** RelRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Body fld+Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** LDH.body fld/SerPl  **Definition:** LDH body fluid to LDH serum ratio aids in determining whether a fluid is an exudate (actively secreted fluid, e.g. due to inflammation), or transudate (passively secreted fluid, due to high hydrostatic pressure or low oncotic pressure). The usual criterion is that a ratio of fluid LDH versus upper limit of normal serum LDH of more than 0.6 or 2⁄3 indicates an exudate, while a ratio of less indicates a transudate.

[75664-3] Human papilloma virus 16 DNA [Presence] in Anorectal specimen by Probe and signal amplification method -- **Component:** Human papilloma virus 16 DNA **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Anorectal **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.sig  **Short name:** HPV16 DNA Anorectl Ql Probe+sig amp  **Definition:** Men who are at high risk for sexually transmitted diseases for HPV-related anal cancer may be tested similar to a cervical Pap smear where the anal lining is swabbed and cells examined microscopically. HPV DNA tests can also be performed on these samples and similarly,  positive results need to be followed up with further testing, including a more thorough exam and possible biopsy. [75666-8] HIV 1+2 Ab and HIV1 p24 Ag [Identifier] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay -- **Component:** HIV 1+2 Ab & HIV1 p24 Ag **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas/Bld **Scale:** Nom **Method:** IA.rapid  **Short name:** HIV 1+2 Ab+HIV1p24 Ag SerPlBld IA.rapid  **Definition:** Simultaneously and separately detects HIV-1 p24 antigen (Ag) and HIV-1/2 antibodies (Ab) in human serum, plasma, capillary, and whole blood. Detecting both HIV-1/2 antibodies and the HIV-1 antigen allows earlier diagnosis of HIV than tests relying solely on the presence of HIV 1/2 antibodies because the antigen may be detectable before the virus. This assay is not intended for newborn screening, cord blood specimens, or on individuals less than 12-years. It is also not intended for use in screening blood products or tissue donors. This term was based on, but not limited in use to, the Alere Determine HIV-1&2 Ag/Ab Combo test kit, which delivers results in 25-30 minutes.

[75694-0] Human papilloma virus 18+45 E6+E7 mRNA [Presence] in Cervix by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Human papilloma virus 18+45 E6+E7 mRNA **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cvx **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** HPV18+45 E6+E7 mRNA Cvx Ql NAA+probe  **Definition:** Detects E6/E7 viral messenger RNA (mRNA) of human papillomavirus (HPV) types 18 and 45 in cervical specimens from women with positive HPV assay results. E6/E7 oncogenes must be expressed for malignant transformation. For women 21-years and older with atypical squamous cells of undetermined significance (ASC-US) cervical cytology results, and HPV positive assay results, this assay can be used to assess the presence or absence of high-risk HPV genotypes 18, and/or 45. For women 30-years and older, the HPV 18, and/or 45 genotype assay can be used to test samples from women with HPV assay positive results, along with cervical cytology results. This assay has not been evaluated for HPV vaccinated patients or in cases of suspected abuse. It does not differentiate HPV 18 and HPV 45. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Aptima HPV 16 18/45 genotype assay which uses target capture, target amplification by transcription-mediated amplification and detection of the amplification products by hybridization protection. To report results for HPV 16 see Human papilloma virus 16 E6+E7 mRNA [Presence] in Cervix by Probe and signal amplification method [LOINC: 82456-5].

[75696-5] Human papilloma virus 31+33+35+39+45+51+52+56+58+59+66+68 DNA [Presence] in Anorectal specimen by Probe and signal amplification method -- **Component:** Human papilloma virus 31+33+35+39+45+51+52+56+58+59+66+68 DNA **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Anorectal **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.sig  **Short name:** HPV HR 12 DNA Anorectl Ql Probe+sig amp  **Definition:** Men who are at high risk for sexually transmitted diseases for HPV-related anal cancer may be tested similar to a cervical Pap smear where the anal lining is swabbed and cells examined microscopically. HPV DNA tests can also be performed on these samples and similarly, positive results need to be followed up with further testing, including a more thorough exam and possible biopsy. There are more than 118 different types of HPV, and approximately 40 different HPVs that can infect the human anogenital mucosa. However, only a subset of approximately 14 of these is considered high-risk for the development of cervical cancer and its precursor lesions. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the cobas HPV test kit which uses PCR and nucleic acid hybridization for the detection of 14 high-risk types.

[75756-7] Bacteria identified in Isolate by MS.MALDI-TOF -- **Component:** Bacteria **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Nom **Method:** MS.MALDI-TOF  **Short name:** Bacteria Islt MS.MALDI-TOF  **Definition:** Bacterial identification by MALDI-TOF (matrix-assisted laser desorption ionization time of flight) is a diagnostic test for identifying the specific organism(s) responsible for a bacterial infection. MALDI-TOF is a form of mass spectrometry that analyzes the proteins contained in an isolated bacterial colony. The resulting mass spectrum is compared to a reference database of bacterial protein mass spectra in order to identify the organism(s) present in the test sample. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Bruker Daltonics MALDI Biotyper CA System, which is indicated for use in diagnosing  Gram-negative bacterial infections.

[75840-9] Total score [PAS] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** PAS  **Definition:** The Patient Activity Scale (PAS), or PAS total score, is derived from the patient's Health Assessment Questionnaire (HAQ), pain, and global severity scores. The PAS is calculated by multiplying the HAQ by 3.33 and then dividing the sum of the pain scale, PtGA and HAQ by 3. Assessment of disease activity for PAS may be defined as remission ≤0.25, minimal ≤3.7, moderate <8.0 and severe ≥8.0.
[75841-7] Total score [PASII] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** PASII  **Definition:** The Patient Activity Scale II (PASII), or PAS-II total score, is derived from the patient's Health Assessment Questionnaire II (HAQ-II), pain, and global severity scores. The PAS-II is calculated by multiplying the HAQ-II by 3.33 and then dividing the sum of the pain scale, PtGA and HAQ-II by 3. Assessment of disease activity for PAS-II may be defined as remission ≤0.25, minimal ≤3.7, moderate <8.0 and severe ≥8.0.
[75842-5] Clinical disease activity index [CDAI] -- **Component:** Clinical disease activity index **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** CDAI  **Definition:** The Clinical Disease Activity Index (CDAI) instrument contains several measurements or scores used to determine rheumatoid arthritis disease activity. The overall CDAI score is calculated using a linear sum of the following: tender 28 joint count, swollen 28 joint count, patient global assessment (PtGA), and physician (or evaluator) global assessment (EGA). The CDAI uses the same measures as the Simple disease activity index (SDAI, [LOINC: 75843-3]) except it does not include the measurement of C-reactive protein levels, an acute phase reactant.
[75843-3] Simplified disease activity index [SDAI] -- **Component:** Simplified disease activity index **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** SDAI  **Definition:** The Simplified (or Simple) Disease Activity Index (SDAI) instrument contains several measurements or scores used to determine rheumatoid arthritis disease activity. The overall SDAI score is calculated using a linear sum of the following: tender 28 joint count, swollen 28 joint count, patient global assessment, physician global assessment, and C-reactive protein levels. The SDAI is similar to the CDAI (Clinical Disease Activity Index, [LOINC: 75842-5]), which uses the same measures as the SDAI except it does not include the measurement of C-reactive protein levels.
[75844-1] Patient global assessment of disease activity - 0-10 numeric rating [Reported] -- **Component:** Patient global assessment of disease activity - 0-10 numeric rating **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Reported  **Definition:** The patient is asked to indicate his/her overall assessment of their disease activity on a scale of 0-10 where 0 is very well and 10 is very poor.
[75845-8] Total score [SDAI] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** SDAI  **Definition:** The Simplified Disease Activity Index (SDAI) is a useful clinical composite score for measuring a patient's rheumatoid arthritis activity in a clinical setting. It is scored by simply adding the numerical values within each of a set of predetermined areas: a twenty eight joint assessment is used to measure tender and swollen joint count; a patient and physician global assessment of disease activity is measured using a visual analogue scale, and finally, levels of the protein C-reactive are measured (mgudl, normal <1 mgudl).

[75858-1] Sterols panel [Mass/volume] - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Sterols panel **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Sterols Pnl SerPl-mCnc  **Definition:** Determination of sterols (e.g. desmosterol, lathosterol, campesterol, and sitosterol) in serum or plasma is performed to diagnose conditions such as desmosterolosis (desmosterol reductase deficiency) and sitosterolemia. [75874-8] Total score [HAQ-DI] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** HAQ-DI  **Definition:** The HAQ-DI score (with aids/devices questions) is calculated as follows: 1) The 8 category scores are added by using the highest sub-category score from each category. 2) If aids/devices and/or help from another person is needed for a given category, the sub-category is adjusted by increasing a zero or a one to a two. If a patient's highest score for that sub-category is a two or three, it remains as is. 3) Divide the summed category scores by the number of categories answered (must be a minimum of 6). The HAQ-DI score will be between 0-3 where 3 is the worst functioning.
[75875-5] Total score [HAQII] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** HAQII  **Definition:** The HAQ-II score is calculated by summing all the scores of each question (a minimum of 7 must be answered) and dividing by the total number of questions answered to get a value between 0 (minimal loss of function) and 3 (completely disabled).
[75876-3] Patient activity scale II [PASII] -- **Component:** Patient activity scale II **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PASII  **Definition:** The Patient Activity Scale II (PAS-II) questionnaire contains a combination of single measures from the Health Assessment Questionnaire II (HAQ-II) as well as the patient's assessment of pain in the past week and global assessment of pain (PtGA). The combined scores from these measures are used to derive the PAS-II total score [LOINC: 75841-7]. The PAS-II is calculated by multiplying the HAQ-II by 3.33 and then dividing the sum of the pain scale, PtGA and HAQ-II by 3. Assessment of disease activity for PAS-II may be defined as remission ≤0.25, minimal ≤3.7, moderate <8.0 and severe ≥8.0. The PAS [LOINC: 75877-1], a longer version, and PAS-II contain only patient derived data and are useful for the overall, continuous measure of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) disease activity.
[75877-1] Patient activity scale [PAS] -- **Component:** Patient activity scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PAS  **Definition:** The Patient Activity Scale (PAS) questionnaire contains a combination of single measures from the Health Assessment Questionnaire (HAQ) as well as the patient's assessment of pain in the past week and global assessment of pain (PtGA). The combined scores from these measures are used to derive the PAS total score [LOINC: 75840-9]. The PAS is calculated by multiplying the HAQ by 3.33 and then dividing the sum of the pain scale, PtGA and HAQ by 3. Assessment of disease activity for PAS may be defined as remission ≤0.25, minimal ≤3.7, moderate <8.0 and severe ≥8.0. The PAS and PAS-II [LOINC: 75875-5] contain only patient derived data and are useful for the overall, continuous measure of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) disease activity.

[75880-5] 14-3-3 eta Ag [Mass/volume] in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** 14-3-3 eta Ag **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** 14-3-3 eta Ag Ser IA-mCnc
[75881-3] Lupus anticoagulant aPTT, dRVVT and PT screening panel W Reflex -- **Component:** Lupus anticoagulant three screening tests W Reflex panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** - **Method:** Coag  **Short name:** LA aPTT+dRVVT+PT W Reflex PPP  **Definition:** The lupus anticoagulant three screen panel is based primarily on the 2009 International Society of Thrombosis and Haemostasis (ISTH) and 2014 Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) guidelines for detecting the presence of lupus anticoagulants (LA), with some modifications based on information from major laboratories that use three screening tests (PT, aPTT and dRVVT) as the first step in LA testing.  The ISTH guidelines recommend using dRVVT as the first screening test and aPTT-LA as the second screening test, followed by reflex to mixing studies with 1:1 Pooled Normal Plasma and confirmatory testing with excess phospholipid (PL) if either of the screening tests are positive. The CLSI guidelines permit use of other screening tests in addition to aPTT and dRVVT, and also recommend changing the order of the tests to screening following by confirmatory and then mixing. Some major laboratories reflex to thrombin time (TT) and reptilase assays if any of the three screening tests have an abnormal result, so both of those tests are included in the panel. Both ISTH and CLSI recommend reporting quantitative results as the ratio of the patient result to the control result for each of the screening, mixing and confirmatory tests that are performed. However, they use different parameters for the control: ISTH recommends calculating the control based on pooled normal plasma (PNP), while CLSI recommends using the mean of the reference interval provided by the assay manufacturer. Many labs report either the actual patient result or the actual patient result and the actual/normal ratio, so we have included both options in the panel. In addition to the quantitative results, both guidelines recommend reporting a qualitative interpretation.
[75882-1] Lupus anticoagulant three screening tests W Reflex [interpretation] -- **Component:** Lupus anticoagulant three screening tests W Reflex **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Coag  **Short name:** LA 3 screen W Reflex-Imp  **Definition:** The answers included in the example answer list are based on the 2014 CLSI guidelines. If none the assays performed suggest the presence of LA, the overall result would be "LA not detected." If all of the assays performed suggest the presence of LA, the overall result would be "LA detected." If the presence or absence of LA cannot be confirmed without repeat testing, the overall result is "LA indeterminate." The 2009 ISTH guidelines include an alternative answer list with the following answers : 1. Findings compatible with the absence of lupus anticoagulants; 2. Findings compatible with the presence of lupus anticoagulants; and 3. To be tested again in 1 week.

[75886-2] HCV antibody screening - Meaningful Use set -- **Component:** HCV antibody screening tests - Meaningful Use set **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** HCV Ab MU Set Patient  **Definition:** Panel for qualitative or quantitative screening for Hepatitis C virus antibodies using any laboratory method except immunoblot assay (IB). This panel was created for Meaningful Use 3 as part of a project by the American Medical Association (AMA) to develop three Clinical Quality Measures for Hepatitis C - 1) One-Time Screening for HCV for Patients at Risk, 2) HCV Screening for Active Injection Drug Users, and 3) Appropriate Screening Follow-up for Patients with HCV Infection. This concept may be used by a physician to document that he/she has reviewed the HCV antibody test results performed by an outside lab. When the physician does not know which specific LOINC code to choose when documenting the test and results from the lab report, this more generic LOINC panel code can be used. The HCV antibody screening panel includes all antibody tests using any method such as EIA but excluding immunoblotting assay (IB) method, since IB is confirmatory Ab test for HCV. In the eMeasure, this LOINC panel and associated LOINCs codes will be included in a larger value set for the specific type of screening - HCV Antibody Test.

[75888-8] HCV RNA screening tests - Meaningful Use set -- **Component:** HCV RNA screening tests - Meaningful Use set **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** HCV RNA MU Set Patient  **Definition:** Panel for qualitative or quantitative screening for Hepatitis C virus RNA. This panel was created for Meaningful Use 3 as part of a project by the American Medical Association (AMA) to develop three Clinical Quality Measures for Hepatitis C - 1) One-Time Screening for HCV for Patients at Risk, 2) HCV Screening for Active Injection Drug Users, and 3) Appropriate Screening Follow-up for Patients with HCV Infection. This concept may be used by physicians to document that he/she has reviewed the HCV RNA test results performed by an outside lab. [75889-6] How often have you had four or more drinks in one day during the past year [Reported] -- **Component:** How often have you had four or more drinks in one D during the past Y **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** 1Y **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Reported  **Definition:** A question asked to determine if the patient is considered a 'risky drinker'. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, an electronic Clinical Quality Measure (eCQM) to be used in the CMS EHR Incentive Program for Meaningful Use. The eCQM allows for multiple ways to screen for alcohol misuse, including AUDIT-C [LOINC: 75626-2] or one of two questions that assess whether the patient consumes alcohol [LOINC: 75855-7] and the frequency of heavy drinking days (this LOINC). [75890-4] Abbreviated Injury Scale predot code AAAM -- **Component:** Abbreviated Injury Scale predot code **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** AAAM  **Short name:** AIS PreDot code AAAM  **Definition:** The Abbreviated Injury Scale (AIS©) predot code that reflects the patient's injuries. The predot code is the six digits preceding the decimal point of an associated AIS severity code, shown as 12(34)(56).7 where 1 = body region, 2 = type of anatomical structure, 3,4 = specific anatomical structure, 5,6 = level of injury and 7 = severity score based on predot code. For example, a femoral shaft fracture may be coded as 851814.3 where 851814 if the numerical identifier (predot code) and 3 is the AIS severity score [LOINC: 75891-2]. The predot code is further defined as 8 = Body Region: Lower Extremity, 5 = Type of Anatomic Structure: Skeletal, 18 = Specific Anatomic Structure: Femur, and 14 = Level of injury: Shaft.
[75891-2] Abbreviated Injury Scale severity score AAAM -- **Component:** Abbreviated Injury Scale severity score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** AAAM  **Short name:** AIS severity score AAAM  **Definition:** The Abbreviated Injury Scale (AIS©) predot code [LOINC: 75890-4] is used to determine the AIS severity score, a 6 point ordinal scale where 1 = minor, 2 = moderate, 3 = serious, 4 = severe, 5 = critical, and 6 = maximal (injury is currently untreatable). The AIS severity score indicates the relative risk of "threat to life" in an average person who sustains the AIS coded injury as his or her only injury.

[75931-6] Inspiration/Expiration time Ratio -- **Component:** Inspiration/Expiration **Property:** TRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Respiratory system **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Insp/Exp time Ratio  **Definition:** Inspiration/expiration time ratio is the ratio of the length of time for inspiration to the length of time for expiration in a single respiratory cycle (inspiration plus expiration). [76064-5] Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score confidence interval by VectraDA -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease activity score confidence interval **Property:** ScoreRange **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** VectraDA  **Short name:** RA DAS CI VectraDA  **Definition:** The disease activity score (DAS) confidence interval range (e.g. 44.5 - 49.5) reflects the precision of a patient's DAS score (e.g. 47) based on repeat measurements. [76065-2] 6p25 and 6q23 and 11q and 8q24 and 9p21 chromosome partial aneuploidy in Blood or Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** 6p25 & 6q23 & 11q & 8q24 & 9p21 chromosome partial aneuploidy **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** 6p25+6q23+11q+8q24+9p21 aneup Bld/T FISH  **Definition:** Malignant melanoma often exhibits abnormal copy number of certain chromosomal regions. The assessment of gains and losses in 6p25, 6q23 11q, 8q24 and 9p21 by fluorescent in situ hybridization (FISH) has been found to be helpful in distinguishing benign from malignant melanocytic lesions. This term was created for the submitter's assay which uses three commercially available probes sets: RREB1/D6Z1/MYB/CCND1 (assessing chromosomes 6 and 11), CDKN2A/D9Z1 (assessing chromosome 9p), and D8Z2/MYC (assessing chromosome 8q). The chromosomal abnormalities in melanoma can be varied, so the combined assay has been found to have a better chance to aid in diagnosis.  The findings have some mechanistic implications, with tumor suppressor genes on 9p and oncogene (MYC) on 8q.

[7613-3] Pistachio IgE Ab [Units/volume] in Serum -- **Component:** Pistacia vera Ab.IgE **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Pistachio IgE Qn
[76133-8] Sugar absorption test panel [Molar ratio] - 5 hour Urine -- **Component:** Sugar absorption test panel **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** 5H **System:** Urine **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Sugar absorption test Pnl 5h Ur-sRto  **Definition:** A non-invasive test that measures the permeability of the small bowel after ingestion of a sugar solution (e.g. lactulose and mannitol).[PMID: 7714684] The test consists of fasting, drinking a sugar solution and collecting the urine specimen for five hours. The submitter's lab measures glucose, sucrose, lactulose, mannitol, and inositol.free (myoinositol) levels in the urine. Results are used as a diagnostic aid for a variety of gastrointestinal and auto-immune diseases, including allergies, autism, hyperactivity and poor concentration, coeliac disease, malabsorption and deficiencies, malnutrition, chronic skin problems, and inflammatory bowel disease in both children and adults. The test may also be used for evaluation of therapeutic interventions.

This test is used as a diagnostic aid for a variety of gastrointestinal diseases and evaluation of therapeutic interventions in both children and adults.[PMID: 7714684]

[76346-6] Microorganism identified in Isolate by MS.MALDI-TOF -- **Component:** Microorganism **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Nom **Method:** MS.MALDI-TOF  **Short name:** Microorganism Islt MS.MALDI-TOF  **Definition:** Microorganism identification by MALDI-TOF (matrix-assisted laser desorption ionization time of flight) is a diagnostic test for identifying the specific microorganism(s), i.e., bacteria and/or fungi, responsible for an infection. MALDI-TOF is a form of mass spectrometry that analyzes the proteins contained in an isolated colony. The resulting mass spectrum is compared to a reference database of bacterial and fungal protein mass spectra in order to identify the organism(s) present in the test sample. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, bioMerieux's VITEK MS system, which is indicated for use to aid in the diagnosis of bacterial and fungal infections.
[76347-4] Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S)/Cortisol [Molar ratio] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate/Cortisol **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** DHEA-S/Cortis SerPl-sRto  **Definition:** Dehydroepiandrosterone-S (DHEA-S) and cortisol are both hormones that are produced by the adrenal glands and secreted in response to stress. DHEA-S/cortisol ratio may indicate the degree to which an individual is buffered against the negative effects of stress. This ratio has been used as an index of adrenocortical function to identify patients with panic disorder, as they may have significantly higher values. [PMID: 2527376]

[76349-0] clonazePAM [Mass/mass] in Meconium by Confirmatory method -- **Component:** clonazePAM **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Meconium **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Confirm  **Short name:** clonazePAM Mec Cfm-mCnt  **Definition:** Meconium begins to form in a fetus's gastrointestinal tract by the end of the first trimester of pregnancy. After birth, infants pass meconium stools for the first two to three days of life, at which time they begin to pass breast milk and/or formula stools. Meconium drug testing reflects maternal drug use during at least the last three months of pregnancy, and possibly during the last four to five months.

[76351-6] Methotrexate polyglutamates [Moles/volume] in Red Blood Cells -- **Component:** Methotrexate polyglutamates **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** RBC **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** MTX-PGs RBC-sCnc
[76352-4] Testosterone/Cortisol [Molar ratio] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Testosterone/Cortisol **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Testost/Cortis SerPl-sRto  **Definition:** The testosterone/cortisol ratio (T/C ratio) is an indicator of recovery from exercise or over-training, and there is some suggestion in the literature of an association between an elevated T/C ratio and aggressive behavior. Testosterone is released following a series of a hormonal processes that begin in the pituitary gland, and its effects include increasing muscle protein synthesis and reducing the catabolic effects of cortisol. Cortisol is released by the adrenal gland under conditions of high physical and mental stress and high temperature, and its effects include reducing growth hormone and testosterone output, reducing muscle and increasing abdominal fat, and impairing immunity, learning and memory.

[76435-7] Patient identifier -- **Component:** Identifier **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Pt ID  **Definition:** The patient identifier is a unique alphanumeric string that identifies a specific patient and is assigned by a specific organization (the assigning authority) that should be reported using [LOINC: 76698-0]. In HL7 v2 messages, the patient identifier is reported in PID-3.1 and the assigning authority in PID-3.4. Examples of patient identifiers are medical record number, driver's license number and Social Security number, and their corresponding assigning authorities are the appropriate healthcare facility, state motor vehicle administration and the Social Security Administration, respectively.

[76471-2] Organization episode of care unique identifier -- **Component:** Organization episode of care unique identifier **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Organization EOC unique ID  **Definition:** The organization episode of care identifier is the unique alphanumeric identifier assigned by a healthcare provider to a given patient for a specific episode of care. An episode of care begins with the initial visit for care for a particular problem and ends when the patient is no longer continuing treatment for that problem. One episode of care can include just one visit or many visits, depending on how long it takes for the problem to resolve. If the patient seeks care for a different problem in the future, a new episode of care identifier will be assigned.

[76478-7] Acetylcholinesterase panel - Amniotic fluid -- **Component:** Acetylcholinesterase panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Amnio fld **Scale:** -  **Short name:** AChE Pnl Amn  **Definition:** Amniotic fluid acetylcholinesterase (AChE) detection panel to aid in diagnosing fetal open neural tube and ventral wall defects that develop in the early embryonic period. Fetal hemoglobin reflex testing is performed to rule out false positive AChE levels that may have been caused by specimen contamination with fetal blood. The patient's gestational age must be at least 13 weeks for accurate AChE detection, with the optimal gestational age ranging between 14 to 18 weeks.
[76479-5] Acetylcholinesterase [Interpretation] in Amniotic fluid Narrative -- **Component:** Acetylcholinesterase **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Amnio fld **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** AChE Amn-Imp  **Definition:** An interpretation of the acetylcholinesterase (AChE) in amniotic fluid testing result which is used as an aid to diagnose open neural tube defect (NDT) and ventral wall defects. The two most common neural tube defects are spina bifida and anencephaly. When an NTD is suspected based upon maternal serum alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) screening results, or diagnosed via ultrasound, detection of acetylcholinesterase (AChE) in amniotic fluid is a useful diagnostic tool as normal amniotic fluid does not contain AChE, unless from the fetus as a result of an open NTD. The patient's gestational age must be at least 13 weeks (second trimester) for accurate AChE detection. Optimal gestational age is 14 to 18 weeks. If acetylcholinesterase is present genetic consultation and high-resolution ultrasound may be suggested.

[76506-5] Social connection and isolation panel -- **Component:** Social connection and isolation panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Definition:** The Social connection and isolation panel is a set of five questions used to assess the number of types of social relationships on which a patient is connected and not isolated. Individuals receive one point for each of the following: being married or living together with someone in a partnership at the time of questioning, averaging three or more social interactions per week, attending church or other religious services more than four times per year, and reporting that they belong to a club or organization. A score of 0 (a summation of social isolation risks) represents the highest level of social isolation and a score of 4 represents the lowest level of social isolation. Among both men and women, a low social isolation score was associated with a mortality hazard ratio similar to that related to smoking and greater than that related to the other traditional clinical risk factors.[PMID: 24028260] This study used the original NHANES III marital status question with an answer list that included 'Married - spouse in household' and 'Married - spouse not in household'. This panel includes the more common and recent marital status term since the answer list is more representative of what is being asked today.

[76512-3] Social isolation score [NHANES] -- **Component:** Social isolation score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NHANES  **Definition:** Social isolation scores range from 0 to 4, with 0 representing the highest level of social isolation and 4 representing the lowest level. Scores of  0 or 1 are the most socially isolated participants. [PMID: 23969421] (PMID: 19189781] Evidence from the CARDIA study demonstrated the value of measuring the difficulty of paying for basics over time (e.g., financial resource strain), because there appeared to be a cumulative effect (e.g., incident hypertension).

[76542-0] Do you feel stress - tense, restless, nervous, or anxious, or unable to sleep at night because your mind is troubled all the time - these days [OSQ] -- **Component:** Do you feel stress - tense, restless, nervous, or anxious, or unable to sleep at night because your mind is troubled all the time - these days **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** OSQ  **Definition:** A single-question stress measure converged with items on psychological symptoms and sleep disturbances and with validated measures of well-being scored on a five-point Likert scale.[PMID: 14712852] The question reads as follows:  Stress means a situation in which a person feels tense, restless, nervous, or anxious, or is unable to sleep at night because his/her mind is troubled all the time. Do you feel this kind of stress these days? where 1 = "not at all," 2 = "only a little," 3 = "to some extent," 4 = "rather much," and 5 = "very much"  This question has been primarily tested in Scandinavian populations and can be linked to the PROMIS Emotional Distress (Depression and Anxiety) Short Form scales, which also are reliable and valid ways to assess stress. As of 2015, there is no clinical cutoff for determining when interventions, such as referral to stress management, are warranted.
[76543-8] Weight management summary report -- **Component:** Weight management summary report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Weight management sum rpt  **Definition:** A weight management (healthy weight) summary report document contains a collection of patient information (e.g. demographics, physical measurements [e.g. BMI], behaviors [e.g. dietary, physical activity, sleep], medications, procedures/interventions, personal goals, and family history) used to assess and monitor healthy weight management. Patient healthy weight data may also be reported to the public health for monitor and surveillance and to establish community programs, services, and facilities to encourage improved healthy lifestyles. Reports based on these data can be used to quantitatively evaluate the impact of program interventions and track changes in BMI and other healthy weight metric prevalence at points-in-time and over-time.
[76544-6] Breast cancer 10-year risk of distant recurrence score Calculated by Prosigna -- **Component:** Breast cancer 10Y risk of distant recurrence score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.Prosigna  **Short name:** Brst ca 10Y dist recur score Prosigna  **Definition:** The 10-year breast cancer distant recurrence risk score (also referred to as ROR Score or Risk of Recurrence Score) is a numerical score on a scale of 0 -100 that is calculated using a statistical model that includes: 1) the results from a 50-gene expression assay performed on formalin fixed paraffin embedded breast tumor tissue previously diagnosed as invasive carcinoma; 2) proliferation score; and 3) gross tumor size. The calculated score and information about lymph node status are used together to classify the patient into a risk category for distant breast cancer recurrence at 10 years. This term was created for the Prosigna™ Breast Cancer Prognostic Gene Signature assay, which is intended for use in postmenopausal women with node-negative (Stage I or II) or node-positive (Stage II), hormone receptor-positive (HR+) breast cancer who have undergone surgery and are to be treated with adjuvant endocrine therapy alone.
[76545-3] Breast cancer 10-year risk of distant recurrence classification [Interpretation] Qualitative by Prosigna -- **Component:** Breast cancer 10Y risk of distant recurrence classification **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Prosigna  **Short name:** Brst ca 10Y dist recur class Prosigna  **Definition:** The risk classification is an ordinal risk category (low, intermediate, high) that indicates a patient's risk of recurrence of breast cancer at 10 years. The classification is based on the 10-year risk of distant recurrence score and lymph node status. Low risk is associated with a 3% chance (confidence interval 2-6%), intermediate risk is associated with a 10% chance (confidence interval 7-14%), and high risk with a 16% chance (confidence interval 11-22%) of distant breast cancer recurrence at 10 years, respectively. [PMID: 24347518] This term was created for the Prosigna™ Breast Cancer Prognostic Gene Signature assay, which is intended for use in postmenopausal women with node-negative (Stage I or II) or node-positive (Stage II), hormone receptor-positive (HR+) breast cancer who have undergone surgery and are to be treated with adjuvant endocrine therapy alone.
[76546-1] Breast cancer 10-year risk of distant recurrence panel by Prosigna -- **Component:** Breast cancer 10Y risk of distant recurrence panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Prosigna  **Short name:** Brst ca 10Y risk dist recur Pnl Prosigna  **Definition:** A 10-year breast cancer distant recurrence risk panel created for the Prosigna™ Breast Cancer Prognostic Gene Signature assay, which is intended for use in postmenopausal women with node-negative (Stage I or II) or node-positive (Stage II), hormone receptor-positive (HR+) breast cancer who have undergone surgery and are to be treated with adjuvant endocrine therapy alone. The panel includes terms for primary tumor size, lymph node status, distant recurrence risk score, and distance recurrence risk classification. The 10-year breast cancer distant recurrence risk score (also referred to as ROR Score or Risk of Recurrence Score) is a numerical score on a scale of 0 -100 that is calculated using a statistical model that includes: 1) the results from a 50-gene expression assay performed on formalin fixed paraffin embedded breast tumor tissue previously diagnosed as invasive carcinoma; 2) proliferation score; and 3) gross tumor size. The calculated score and information about lymph node status are used together to classify the patient into a risk category for distant breast cancer recurrence at 10 years.

[76585-9] Wound microorganism gene identification panel by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Wound microorganism gene identification panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** Wnd micro gene id pnl Patient NAA+probe  **Definition:** This panel includes up to three terms with different properties for each individual component in order to accommodate variations in reporting based on specimen type. [76632-9] Enterovirus sp [Presence] in Isolate by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Enterovirus sp **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IF  **Short name:** Entero sp Islt Ql IF  **Definition:** Enteroviruses include polio, coxsackie and echoviruses and are in the Picornaviridae family. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Light Diagnostics Pan-Enterovirus detection kit, which can detect the presence of 67 numbered enterovirus types, including 31 echoviruses, 23 coxsackie A and 6 coxsackie B viruses, 3 polioviruses and 4 other enteroviruses.
[76634-5] Q-T interval corrected based on Fridericia formula -- **Component:** QTc interval **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Fridericia formula  **Short name:** Q-T interval corrected Fridericia  **Definition:** The purpose of calculating a corrected QT interval (QTc) is to normalize each individual QT measurement from a given study to a single measurement that would represent the QT interval if the patient had a heart rate of 60 beats per minute. Several formulas are available to calculate QTc, including the Bazett formula and the Fridericia formula. Bazett's formula is used most commonly, but for situations in which the patient's heart rate is significantly elevated, the Fridericia formula may yield a more accurate QTc that reflects the QTc that would have been obtained were the patient in normal sinus rhythm. [PMID: 23972343] To report a QTc without specifying a method, use LOINC code 8636-3 QT interval corrected. To report QTc calculated using the Bazett formula, use LOINC code 76635-2.
[76635-2] Q-T interval corrected based on Bazett formula -- **Component:** QTc interval **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Bazett formula  **Short name:** Q-T interval corrected Bazett  **Definition:** The purpose of calculating a corrected QT interval (QTc) is to normalize each individual QT measurement from a given study to a single measurement that would represent the QT interval if the patient had a heart rate of 60 beats per minute. Several formulas are available to calculate QTc, including the Bazett formula and the Fridericia formula. Bazett's formula is used most commonly, but for situations in which the patient's heart rate is significantly elevated, the Fridericia formula may yield a more accurate QTc that reflects the QTc that would have been obtained were the patient in normal sinus rhythm. [PMID: 23972343] To report a QTc without specifying a method, use LOINC code 8636-3 QT interval corrected. To report QTc calculated using the Fridericia formula, use LOINC code 76634-5.

[76640-2] Coronary artery catheterization study -- **Component:** Coronary artery catheterization study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Coronary arteries **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Coronary aa cath study  **Definition:** This term represents a coronary angiography study. Coronary angiography is a type of cardiac catheterization study in which a dye is injected into the bloodstream in order to visualize the coronary arteries that supply blood to the heart and see if any of the arteries are partially or completely blocked. [PMHID: 18367060]

[76642-8] Percutaneous coronary intervention study -- **Component:** Percutaneous coronary intervention study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Cardiac catheterization  **Short name:** Perc coronary interv study  **Definition:** This term represents the percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) procedure. PCI, also known as coronary angioplasty, is a procedure performed via cardiac catheterization in order to open up the narrowed or blocked blood vessels (i.e., coronary arteries) that supply blood to the heart. The procedure may involve balloon catheter inflation and/or stent placement in the affected portion(s) of the coronary arteries. [PMID: 32310583]

[76661-8] Sex of Provider [NMMDS] -- **Component:** Sex **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Provider **Scale:** Nom **Method:** NMMDS  **Definition:** Sex of the provider, created for the Nursing Management Minimum Data Set (NMMDS) "Gender" concept. [76662-6] Instructions [Text] Medication Narrative -- **Component:** Instructions **Property:** Txt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Medication **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Instructions Medication  **Definition:** Sig's are the provider's instructions on how to take a medication. They usually follow immediately after the drug name and strength on a written prescription. "Sig" is a shortened version of the Latin word Signetur, meaning "let it be labeled." The following prescriptions contain two examples of Sigs, starting with "take...": Thyroxin 150 ug, take one tab by mouth every morning or Azitrhrommcyin 5 day pack, take 500 mg (2 tabs) on day one and 1 tab (250 mg) on day 2 through 5. May take with food but not antacids This term was created for HL7's Consolidated CDA (C-CDA) to explicitly identify the free text Sig within each medication. The C-CDA is a library of clinical CDA templates named in Meaningful Use regulation to support transition of care. The availability of the free text Sig makes it easier for providers to write prescriptions and get a compact and readable list of their patients' active medications in a grid format with one column for the drug name and strength, another column for the sig, and (usually) fitting one active medication per row.

[76665-9] Protein [Mass/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid --1st tube -- **Component:** Protein^1st tube **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Prot tube1 CSF-mCnc  **Definition:** A sample of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is collected using a procedure called a lumbar puncture or spinal tap. Often, three or more separate tubes of CSF are collected (tube 1, tube 2, tube 3), and multiple tests may be run on the different tubes. Only a small amount of protein is normally present in CSF because proteins are large molecules and do not cross the blood/brain barrier easily. Decreases in CSF protein are not generally considered significant. Increases in protein are most commonly seen with; meningitis and brain abscesses, brain or spinal cord tumors, multiple sclerosis and other disorders. [Lab Tests Online]

[76669-1] Glucose [Moles/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid --1st tube -- **Component:** Glucose^1st tube **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Glucose tube1 CSF-sCnc  **Definition:** A sample of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is collected using a procedure called a lumbar puncture or spinal tap. Often, three or more separate tubes of CSF are collected (tube 1, tube 2, tube 3), and multiple tests may be run on the different tubes. Normal CSF glucose is about 2/3 the concentration of blood glucose. Glucose levels may decrease when cells not normally present metabolize glucose. This may include bacteria or cells present due to inflammation (WBCs) or shed by tumors. [Lab Tests Online]

This may include bacteria or cells present due to inflammation (WBCs) or shed by tumors. [Lab Tests Online]
[76673-3] Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid by Manual count --1st tube -- **Component:** Erythrocytes^1st tube **Property:** NCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Manual count  **Short name:** RBC tube1 # CSF Manual  **Definition:** A sample of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is collected using a procedure called a lumbar puncture or spinal tap. Often, three or more separate tubes of CSF are collected (tube 1, tube 2, tube 3), and multiple tests may be run on the different tubes. Normally no erythrocytes are present in CSF. The presence of red blood cells may indicate bleeding into the CSF or may indicate a "traumatic tap" - blood that leaked into the CSF sample during collection. [Lab Tests Online]

A significant increase in white blood cells in the CSF is seen with infection or inflammation of the central nervous system.

[76679-0] Color of Cerebral spinal fluid--1st tube -- **Component:** Color^1st tube **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Color tube1 CSF  **Definition:** A sample of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is collected using a procedure called a lumbar puncture or spinal tap. Often, three or more separate tubes of CSF are collected, and multiple tests may be run on the different tubes. Normal CSF is clear and colorless. Changes in the color of the CSF are not diagnostic but may point to additional substances in the fluid. Yellow, orange, or pink CSF may indicate the breakdown of blood cells due to bleeding into the CSF or the presence of bilirubin. Green CSF may also sometimes be seen with bilirubin or infection. [Lab Tests Online]

[76693-1] Bacteria identified in Blood product unit.autologous by Culture -- **Component:** Bacteria **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^BPU.autologous **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Culture  **Short name:** Bacteria BPU.autologous Cult  **Definition:** Autologous blood donations are donations that individuals give for their own use, for example before a surgery. Autologous blood can be bacterially contaminated much like allogeneic blood and cause a transfusion reaction. Culturing the autologous blood unit may be a part of the transfusion reaction workup protocol.  Whether the blood unit was autologous and or allogeneic is important information for the transfusion reaction investigation by revealing the collection and transfusion processes and workflow that may have allowed the unit to become contaminated. [PMID: 9563411].

[77008-1] Human coronavirus 229E+OC43 RNA [Presence] in Nasopharynx by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Human coronavirus 229E+OC43 RNA **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Nph **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** HCoV 229E+OC43 RNA Nph Ql NAA+probe  **Definition:** Human coronavirus 229E is a single-stranded, positive-sense, RNA virus species in the Alphacoronavirus genus of the subfamily Coronavirinae, in the family Coronaviridae, of the order Nidovirales. Human coronavirus OC43 is an enveloped, positive-sense, single-stranded RNA virus species in the Betacoronavirus genus, of the subfamily Coronavirinae, in the family Coronaviridae, of the order Nidovirales. [Wikipedia] This assay tests for Human coronavirus 229E and OC43 RNA but does not distinguish between them.
[77009-9] Porphyrin fractions panel [Moles/volume] - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Porphyrin fractions panel **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Porphyrin Fract Pnl SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Determination of porphyrin profile in serum or plasma is performed to evaluate porphyria. As with urine, it is most useful to measure the total amount of porphyrins. If increased, then the laboratory may determine which porphyrins predominate to identify the presence and nature of the porphyrin defect. A summary of the different porphyria disorders, the genes where mutations occur and the associated symptoms is given in: http://www.porphyria-europe.com/02-for-healthcare/the-porphyrias.asp.
[77010-7] Urea [Molar ratio] in Serum or Plasma --post dialysis/pre dialysis -- **Component:** Urea^post dialysis/pre dialysis **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Urea post/pre dialysis SerPl-sRto  **Definition:** The urea reduction ratio (URR) is a measure of urea clearance during hemodialysis and is calculated based on pre- and post-hemodialysis measurements of blood urea. The URR  is directly associated with survival for patients on chronic hemodialysis (a higher URR is associated with lower risk of mortality). Urea clearance is routinely recorded and used to monitor the adequacy of hemodialysis and to compare the quality of hemodialysis delivery across dialysis facilities. [PMID: 10727547]

[77161-8] Prothrombin activity [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay --immediately after addition of factor II depleted plasma -- **Component:** Prothrombin.activity^immediately after addition of factor II depleted plasma **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Coag  **Short name:** Prothrom imm FII DP PPP-aCnc  **Definition:** Tests for congenital or secondary deficiency of prothrombin (also called FII or factor II) by measuring prothrombin activity in patient plasma using a modified prothrombin time test (PT). Patient plasma is diluted and then added to a plasma deficient in prothrombin. Correction of the clotting time of the deficient plasma is proportional to the concentration (% activity) of that factor in the patient plasma, interpolated from a calibration curve. Prothrombin is a vitamin-K dependent plasma protein made in the liver and circulated in plasma. Congenital deficiency is very rare and usually causes minor bleeding. Secondary disorders are liver diseases, hyperfibrinolysis, disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), oral anticoagulant therapy, or vitamin-K deficiency. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the HemolL Factor II deficient plasma test kit. Results may be expressed in % activity, IU/ml, or seconds.

[77165-9] Anaplasma phagocytophilum and Ehrlichia chaffeensis IgG panel [Titer] - Serum -- **Component:** Anaplasma phagocytophilum & Ehrlichia chaffeensis IgG panel **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn  **Short name:** A phagocyt + E chaff Ab.IgG Pnl Titr Ser  **Definition:** Ehrlichiosis is a general name to describe several bacterial diseases affecting humans and animals that are transmitted by tick bites. In the United States, the term "ehrlichiosis" may be broadly applied to several different infections. Ehrlichia chaffeensis and Ehrlichia ewingii are transmitted by the lonestar tick in the southeastern and southcentral United States. In addition, a third Ehrlichia species provisionally called Ehrlichia muris-like (EML) has been identified in a small number of patients residing in or traveling to Minnesota and Wisconsin; a tick vector for the EML organism has not yet been established. The symptoms caused by infection with these Ehrlichia species usually develop 1-2 weeks after being bitten by an infected tick. The tick bite is usually painless, and about half of the people who develop ehrlichiosis may not even remember being bitten by a tick. [CDC: Ehrlichiosis] This panel contains IgG antibody tests for two infecting species, E. charffeensis and A. phagocytophilum.

[77173-3] Hepatocyte growth factor receptor Ag [Presence] in Tissue by Immune stain -- **Component:** Hepatocyte growth factor receptor Ag **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Immune stain  **Short name:** HGFR Ag Tiss Ql ImStn  **Definition:** The stain is interpreted as positive if 50% or more of the tumor cells show moderate to strong (2+, 3+) membranous staining, indicating MET amplification. MET amplification (positive) has been seen in approximately 22% of non-small-cell lung cases with acquired resistance to anti-EGFR inhibitors. Recent Phase II clinical trails (OAM4558g) results were released at ASCO in 2011 showed that patients who were c-MET positive (2+ or 3+) lived twice as long without disease progression (PFS) when they received MetMAb therapy in combination with erlotinib versus receiving erlotinib alone.
[77174-1] CALR gene exon 9 mutations found [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method Nominal -- **Component:** CALR gene exon 9 targeted mutation analysis **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CALR Exon 9 Mut Anl Bld/T
[77175-8] Coxiella burnetii phase 1 and 2 IgG and IgM panel - Serum -- **Component:** Coxiella burnetii phase 1 & 2 Ab.IgG & IgM panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** C burnet Ph 1 + 2 IgG + IgM Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel combines assays for phase 1 and 2, IgG and IgM antibody testing, for diagnosing past and present Q fever infections caused by Coxiella burnetii. Coxiella burnetii is a species of intracellular, pathogenic bacteria, and is the causative agent of Q fever. The genus Coxiella is morphologically similar to the rickettsia, but with a variety of genetic and physiological differences. C. burnetii are small Gram negative bacteria with two growth phases, as well as a spore form which lies idle in soil. It can survive standard disinfectants, and is resistant to many other environmental changes. Pathogenesis:The ID50 (the dose need to infect 50% of experimental subjects) is one via inhalation i.e. inhalation of one organism will yield disease in 50% of the population. Disease occurs in two states: An acute state presents with headaches, chills, and respiratory symptoms, and an insidious chronic stage. While most infections clear up spontaneously, treatment with tetracycline appears to reduce the symptomatic duration and reduce the likelihood of chronic infection.
[77176-6] Hepatitis B virus little e Ag and Ab panel - Serum -- **Component:** Hepatitis B virus little e Ag & Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** -  **Short name:** HBV e Ag + Ab Pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel is used to determine the infectivity of hepatitis B virus carriers and to monitor the serologic response of chronically infected patients on antiviral therapy. There is still no complete cure for hepatitis B, there are 7 approved drugs for adults (2 for children) and many promising new drugs in development. Current treatments seem to be most effective in those who show signs of active liver disease. Some of the approved Hepatitis B Drugs in the United States are: Interferon Alpha, Lamivudine, Entecavir, Telbivudine and Tenofovir. [From the Hepatitis B Foundation,[http://www.hepb.org/patients/hepatitis_b_treatment.htm]

[77217-8] Salivary gland protein 1 and Carbonic anhydrase 6 and Parotid secretory protein IgA and IgG and IgM panel - Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Salivary gland protein 1 & Carbonic anhydrase 6 & Parotid secretory protein Ab.IgA & IgG & IgM panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** SP-1+CA6+PSP IgA+IgG+IgM Pnl Ser IA  **Definition:** Sjogren's disease is an autoimmune disease associated with kidney and lung disease as well as disorders of the salivary and lacrimal glands, which lead to problems with dry eyes and mouth. Sjogren's syndrome is associated with isolated salivary and lacrimal gland dysfunction and may be associated with other autoimmune disorders, such as systemic lupus erythematosus. Antibodies to salivary gland protein 1 (SP-1), carbonic anhydrase 6 (CA6) and parotid secretory protein (PSP) may be useful markers for identifying patients with Sjogren's disease or Sjogren's syndrome early in the course of disease and for those who have significant symptoms but are anti-Ro and anti-La antibody negative, which are two of the antibodies that are typically used to diagnose Sjogren's. Antibodies to SP-1, CA6 and PSP rarely occur in healthy controls or patients with other autoimmune disorders who do not have eye and mouth symptoms. [PMID: 23123440] This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the IMMCO Diagnostics' early Sjogren's syndrome antibody profile, which includes terms for IgA, IgG and IgM antibodies to SP-1, CA6 and PSP.

[77251-7] Percent impairment - confidence [OPTIMAL] -- **Component:** Percent impairment - confidence **Property:** Prctl **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** OPTIMAL  **Definition:** The OPTIMAL percent impairment should be calculated independently for difficulty and confidence questions. [77253-3] Microalbumin/Creatinine [Ratio] in Urine by Detection limit <= 1.0 mg/L -- **Component:** Albumin/Creatinine **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Detection limit <= 1.0 mg/L  **Short name:** Microalbumin/Creat Ur DL<=1mg/L-Rto  **Definition:** Measures the albumin excretion in urine to aid in the early detection of kidney disease (and increased risk of cardiovascular events) for patients with diabetes. Normal 24 hour urine albumin excretion is less than 30 mg/day in adults.  24-hour urine excretion results between 30-300 mg/day are defined as microalbuminuria (MAU) which may occur with kidney damage. While a 24-hour urine is preferred, a random urine sample can be tested as well.

[77281-4] Stability rate [NMMDS] -- **Component:** Nursing staff at beginning who remained/Mean number of nursing staff **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** {Nursing unit}.{Job position} **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NMMDS  **Definition:** The percent of nursing staff employed by job position at the beginning of the reporting period and who still stayed at the end of the period. [77282-2] Instability rate [NMMDS] -- **Component:** Nursing staff at beginning who left/Mean number of nursing staff **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** {Nursing unit}.{Job position} **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NMMDS  **Definition:** The percent of nursing staff employed by job position at the beginning of the reporting period but who left the unit or service at the end of the period (i.e. instability rate). It is calculated by dividing the number of nursing staff at beginning by position leaving during the reporting period divided by the number of nursing staff by job position at the start of the period.

[77293-9] Days per week of moderate to vigorous physical activity -- **Component:** Ds per W of moderate to vigorous physical activity **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Definition:** The Center for Disease Control (CDC) defines physical activity as "anything that gets your body moving." On a scale of 1 to 10,  where sitting is a 0 and the highest level of activity is a 10, moderate physical activity is a 5 or 6 and vigorous activity is a 7 or 8. When a child or adolescent does moderate physical activity, their heart will beat faster than normal and they will breathe harder than normal. When they do vigorous activity, their heart will beat much faster than normal and they will breathe much harder than normal. (http://www.cdc.gov/physicalactivity/everyone/guidelines/children.html, Accessed April 16, 2015).

[77344-0] Colony count [#/volume] in Implanted device by Culture -- **Component:** Colony count **Property:** NCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Implanted device **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Culture  **Short name:** CC # Implnt dev Cult  **Definition:** Performed when there is a clinical assessment of prosthetic infection or clinical suspicion of aseptic prosthetic joint loosening. [77348-1] Tacrolimus [Mass/volume] in Blood --trough -- **Component:** Tacrolimus^trough **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Tacrolimus Trough Bld-mCnc
[77349-9] Everolimus [Mass/volume] in Blood by LC/MS/MS --trough -- **Component:** Everolimus^trough **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** LC/MS/MS  **Short name:** Everolimus Trough Bld LC/MS/MS-mCnc  **Definition:** Trough blood levels of everolimus is performed for therapeutic monitoring and dose individualization. Koster et. al. (2009) reported a systematic deviation in blood levels of everolimus (30% lower) measured by LC-MS/MS compared with immunoassays.[PMID: 19065123] Everolimus may be used to treat patients post transplant (e.g, kidney transplant) or with tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) who have subependymal giant cell astrocytoma (SEGA). Maintaining everolimus troughs between 3-8 ng/mL is recommended for patients post transplant. Maintaining trough levels of 5 to 15 ng/mL is recommended in pediatric and adult patients with SEGA.
[77350-7] Erythropoietin (EPO) Ab [Presence] in Serum -- **Component:** Erythropoietin Ab **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** EPO Ab Ser Ql  **Definition:** The test in performed in patients who do not have a clinical response to recombinant erythropoietin (EPO) therapy in order to determine whether they have antibodies to the drug. EPO is used for patients with a variety of disorders, including advanced kidney disease, to raise their hemoglobin level. For patients with EPO antibodies, other therapeutic options are available.
