[77353-1] Noninvasive colorectal cancer DNA and occult blood screening [Interpretation] in Stool Narrative -- **Component:** Noninvasive colorectal cancer DNA+occult blood screening **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stool **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Noninv colon ca DNA+occ bld scrn Stl-Imp  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, Exact Sciences' Cologuard DNA screening test for colorectal cancer. The Cologuard algorithm includes two laboratory procedures performed on a single stool specimen: 1) detection and quantification of fecal occult blood using ELISA; and 2) detection of multiple genetic biomarkers (NDRG4 and BMP3 methylation targets, seven KRAS point mutations, and beta actin as a proxy for the total amount of human DNA in each sample) using magnetic bead based oligonucleotide hybrid capture to isolate the biomarkers followed by quantitative real-time target and signal amplification. The occult blood and genetic biomarker results are subsequently entered into a logistic regression model, and the results from this model are used to calculate a composite score from which the final binary result (positive, negative) is determined. [PMID: 24645800]

[77367-1] Cholinesterase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by calculation --Dibucaine -- **Component:** Cholinesterase^dibucaine **Property:** CCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Cholinesterase Dibucaine SerPl Calc-cCnc  **Definition:** People with genetic variants or acquired deficiencies of plasma cholinesterase that result in lower enzymatic activity can have a very difficult time recovering from surgery. To identify these patients, tests using various inhibitory agents are performed. The most common inhibitors are fluoride and dibucaine. The degree to which the agent inhibits the enzyme (fluoride number or dibucaine number) can be used to predict neuromuscular recovery after surgery. [77374-7] clomiPRAMINE trough, clomiPRAMINE+Norclomipramine trough and Norclomipramine panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** clomiPRAMINE trough & clomiPRAMINE+Norclomipramine trough & Norclomipramine panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Clom, Clom+Nor Trgh + Nor Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** Trough levels of clomipramine and levels of its metabolite, norclomipramine, are measured to monitor therapeutic drug levels and evaluate toxicity of clomipramine, a tricyclic antidepressant used primarily to treat obsessive-compulsive disorders, panic disorders and depression. [77547-8] American National Adult Reading Test (AmNART) panel -- **Component:** American National Adult Reading Test panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Definition:** The American National Adult Reading Test (AmNART) is the American version of the NART, an oral reading test containing words with atypical grapheme to phoneme associations making it difficult to pronounce words phonetically. The test was developed to estimate premorbid intelligence of older adults. The premorbid estimate is used to improve the early diagnosis of dementia.[PMID: 1779032]

[77564-3] Brief Pain Inventory Short Form - pain interference panel -- **Component:** Brief Pain Inventory short form - pain interference panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Definition:** This panel represents the pain interference portion of the Brief Pain Inventory (BPI) Short Form. The patient is asked to describe on a scale from 0-10 how, during the past 24 hours, pain has interfered with their general activity, mood, walking ability, normal work (includes both work outside the home and housework), relations with other people, sleep, and enjoyment of life. A score of 0 indicates pain "does not interfere" and 10 means it "Completely interferes". The BPI Short Form is one of the formats for the BPI and is commonly used in clinical trials. It differs from the BPI Long Form in that it uses a 24-hour recall period versus in the past week. The BPI pain interference is typically scored as the mean of the seven interference item scores. The results are clinically useful if more than 50% (four out of seven) of the total items have been completed.(The Brief Pain Inventory Users Guide, © 2009 Charles S. Cleeland)
[77565-0] Pain interference score [BPI Short Form] -- **Component:** Pain interference score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** BPI.short  **Definition:** The Brief Pain Interference (BPI) short form pain interference is typically scored as the mean of the seven interference item scores for general activity, walking, work, mood, enjoyment of life, relations with others, and sleep. The results are clinically useful if more than 50% (four out of seven) of the total items have been completed. (The Brief Pain Inventory Users Guide, © 2009 Charles S. Cleeland)

[77582-5] International Physical Activity Questionnaire Self-Administered Short Form [IPAQ] -- **Component:** International Physical Activity Questionnaire self-administered short form **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** The International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ) Self-Administered Short Form asks about three specific types of activity: 1. walking; 2. moderate intensity; and 3. vigorous intensity. These activities may be part of your employment, house or yard work, during spare time for recreation, exercise or sport, and transportation (e.g., walking to and from school or work). The IPAQ was designed primarily for population surveillance of physical activity among adults (ages 15-69 years). The items in the short IPAQ form were structured to provide separate scores on walking, moderate-intensity and vigorous-intensity activity. Computation of the total score for the short form is based on summation of the duration (in minutes) and frequency (days) of walking, moderate-intensity and vigorous-intensity activities. [https://sites.google.com/site/theipaq/]

[77589-0] During the last 7 days, how much time did you usually spend sitting on a weekday [IPAQ] -- **Component:** During the last 7D, how much time did you usually spend sitting on a weekday **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** The IPAQ sitting question is an additional indicator variable of time spent in sedentary activity and is not included as part of any summary score of physical activity.  Data on sitting should be reported as median values and interquartile ranges.
[77591-6] Walking physical activity [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Walking physical activity **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** A patient's total walking physical activity can be reported as a continuous variable based on the duration (e.g. in minutes) and frequency (e.g. days) of walking over the past 7 days. In the International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ) Long form, the patient is asked separate questions on walking in three domains: at work, for transport, and in leisure time. The total duration and frequency for each of these domains is used to calculate the patient's total walking physical activity level. In the IPAQ Short form, these domains are grouped together into a single question for duration and another for frequency of walking over the past 7 days. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested.    Calculation for walking physical activity for the long and short IPAQ forms:  Short form: Walking MET-minutes/week = 3.3 * walking minutes/day * walking days/week   Long form: Walking MET-minutes/week = 3.3 * walking minutes/day (at work+for transport+in leisure) * walking days/week (at work+for transport+in leisure)    For the IPAQ, walking is considered a moderate-intensity activity. To determine a total moderate-intensity MET-minutes/week, the walking and moderate totals should be added.
[77592-4] Moderate physical activity [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Moderate physical activity **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** A patient's total moderate physical activity can be reported as a continuous variable based on the duration (e.g. in minutes) and frequency (e.g. days) of moderate activity over the past 7 days. In the International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ) Long form, the patient is asked separate questions on moderate physical activity in various domains, including for transport (cycling), at work, doing chores (yard & house), and for recreation, sport, exercise or leisure. The total duration and frequency for these domains is used to calculate the patient's moderate physical activity level. In the IPAQ Short form, these domains are grouped together into a single question for duration and another for frequency of moderate physical activity in the last 7 days. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested.    Calculation for moderate physical activity for the long and short IPAQ forms:  Short form: Moderate MET-minutes/week = 4.0 * moderate-intensity activity minutes per day * moderate-intensity days per week     Long form: Moderate MET-minutes/week = Cycle MET-minutes/week (Transport) + Moderate MET-minutes/week (Work + Mod Yard chores + Inside chores + Leisure) + Vigorous MET-minutes/week (Vig Yard chores)   where   Cycle MET-minutes/week = 6.0 * Cycling minutes/day * Cycle days/week for transportation   Moderate MET-minutes/week = 4.0 * total moderate activity minutes/day * total moderate days/week (for Work + Mod Yard chores + Inside chores + Leisure)   Vigorous MET-minutes/week = 5.5 * vigorous-intensity activity minutes/day * vigorous-intensity days/week doing yard work    For the IPAQ, walking is considered a moderate-intensity activity. To determine a total moderate-intensity MET-minutes/week, the walking and moderate totals should be added.
[77593-2] Vigorous physical activity [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Vigorous physical activity **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** A patient's total vigorous physical activity can be reported as a continuous variable based on the duration (e.g. in minutes) and frequency (e.g. days) of vigorous activity over the past 7 days. In the International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ) Long form, the patient is asked separate questions on vigorous physical activity in two domains: at work and in leisure time. The total duration and frequency for these domains is used to calculate the patient's vigorous physical activity level. In the IPAQ Short form, these domains are grouped together into a single question for duration and another for frequency of vigorous physical activity in the last 7 days. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested.    Calculation for vigorous physical activity for the long and short IPAQ forms:  Short form: Vigorous MET-minutes/week = 8.0 * vigorous-intensity activity minutes per day * vigorous-intensity days per week  Long form: Vigorous MET-minutes/week = 8.0 * total vigorous-intensity activity minutes per day (at Work + in Leisure) * total vigorous-intensity days per week (at Work + in Leisure)

[77594-0] Total physical activity [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Total physical activity **Property:** ARat **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** Computation of total weekly physical activity as a continuous variable for the short form requires summation of the duration (in minutes) and frequency (days) of walking, moderate-intensity and vigorous-intensity activities.  Domain specific estimates cannot be estimated. The activity level is reported in MET-minutes/week or MET-minutes/day, although the former is more frequently used and is thus suggested. This quantitative total weekly physical activity result can be transformed into a categorical result using [LOINC: 77595-7].
[77595-7] Total physical activity score [IPAQ] -- **Component:** Total physical activity score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 7D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IPAQ  **Definition:** Total weekly physical activity can be reported categorically as low, moderate or high based on the quantitative physical activity result [LOINC: 77594-0]. The criteria for these levels have been set taking into account that IPAQ asks questions in all domains of daily life, resulting in higher median MET-minutes estimates than would have been estimated from leisure-time participation alone.

[77597-3] Orders placed Document -- **Component:** Orders placed **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Orders placed Doc  **Definition:** Orders placed section contains active and completed (not planned) orders for observations, interventions, encounters, services, and procedures for the patient. Order information is used to validate that clinical activities performed by other providers and suppliers authorized by the responsible provider. The details of orders are provided but not the acts involved in the processing and fulfillment of the order. In general, planned order activity is included under the plan of treatment section (also known as "plan of care") [LOINC: 18776-5]. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: Additional CDA R2 Templates - Clinical Documents for Payers - Set 1 (CPD1), Release 1.

[77601-3] Enhanced note -- **Component:** Enhanced note **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Enhanced note  **Definition:** An enhanced note is generated by a provider at the end of an office visit, consult, or home health encounter with a patient. Enhanced encounters may involve face-to-face time with the patient or may fall under the auspices of tele-medicine visits.  An enhanced note includes all sections relevant to the specific visit, except for details concerning procedures, operations or imaging performed during the encounter. The document is intended to support the entire contents of the medical record related to a specific encounter with a patient for the administrative or clinical exchange with a third party. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Enhanced Encounter Document level template included in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: Additional CDA R2 Templates - Clinical Documents for Payers - Set 1 (CPD1), Release 1.

[77602-1] Research study consent -- **Component:** Research study consent **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Patient  **Short name:** Research study consent  **Definition:** Informed consent document for a patient to participate in a research study. The consent is signed by the patient at the beginning of a (clinical) research study, including observational or interventional research. It contains the study ID, study description and procedures that the patient will undergo during the study.  In addition to a signature, it may contain choices selected by the patient (e.g., later use of collected tissue samples for related research).
[77603-9] Medication treatment plan.extended Document -- **Component:** Medication treatment plan.extended **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Med tx plan.extended Doc  **Definition:** A medication treatment plan (MTP) document is generated when a health care professional (e.g. medical specialist, general practitioner) adds a medication to the medication treatment plan of a patient. The document includes information about a patient's medication treatment plans [LOINC: 77604-7] as well as patient demographics, health care provider information, and authorization information. The MTP document can contain all medications the patient was supposed to take in the past as well as those that are current and active (i.e. should be taken by the patient). The information can be used to make an intake plan for the patient. The plan in itself has no dependency on prescriptions or dispenses, which are related to the logistic process necessary to get the drugs when specific procurement rules apply. This term was originally created for, though not limited in use to, the IHE Medication Treatment Plan profile in the Community Medication Prescription and Dispense set (CMPD).
[77604-7] Medication treatment plan.brief Document -- **Component:** Medication treatment plan.brief **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Med tx plan.brief Doc  **Definition:** A brief medication treatment plan (MTP) is contained within the extended MTP document [LOINC: 77603-9] and contains one or more medication treatment plan items for a patient. The brief MTP describes the medications a patient is taking, will take or has taken as part of a plan. This term was originally created for, though not limited in use to, the IHE MTP profile in the Community Medication Prescription and Dispense (CMPD) set. The MTP profile states MTP items within the MTP section are very close to prescription (PRE) items but differ primarily in the position of the global workflow. While an MTP item is more an information piece (i.e. "this medication is part of the medication treatment plan"), a PRE item represents an instantiated prescription of the same medication. Most of the normative sections of the MTP profile will therefore be identical to the corresponding ones in the PRE profile.
[77605-4] Influenza virus A H5 RNA [Cycle Threshold #] in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Influenza virus A H5 RNA **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** FLUAV H5 RNA Ct Spec Qn NAA+probe  **Definition:** The goose/Guangdong lineage H5 viruses emerged in the late 90s in Asia and subsequently became the only lineage of viruses known to be highly pathogenic to poultry and sustained in wild birds. The World Health Organization recognizes these viruses under a unified H5 clade nomenclature. Viruses from this lineage, specifically clade 2.3.4.4, were introduced into North American flyways in late 2014 via the Bering Strait into the Pacific flyway and again in late 2021 into the Atlantic flyway. [PMID:25855748] The NALHN influenza A molecular assay (matrix 2009) works well for the detection of this viral clade and is the primary surveillance tool. As a screening tool, the NAHLN H5 assay does not distinguish geographic lineage nor pathotype (LPAI vs HPAI). This clade specific assay uses real-time reverse transcriptase PCR targeted to clade H5 2.3.4.4.
[77606-2] Weight-for-length Per age and sex -- **Component:** Weight-for-length **Property:** Prctl **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Per age and sex  **Short name:** Wt-for-len Per age+sex
[77607-0] Cortisol [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --1 minute post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Cortisol^1M post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortis 1M p chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Timed cortisol levels are obtained for adrenocorticotropic hormone challenge tests to assist in diagnosing adrenocortical insufficiency as found in Cushing's syndrome and Addison's disease. There are different types of challenges. ACTH (adrenocorticotropic hormone) stimulation, where cosyntropin (a synthetic form of ACTH) is given, and then blood is sampled for cortisol levels before the bolus, and at specified times after the bolus. The ACTH stimulation directly evaluates adrenal gland function and indirectly evaluates pituitary gland and hypothalamus function. Cortisol levels rise significantly after administration of cosyntropin if the adrenal glands are functioning normally. For the CRH (corticotropin-releasing hormone) stimulation, CRH is given and cortisol and ACTH levels are determined before the injection, at the injection time, and specified times following injection. The CRH stimulation test is used to distinguish Cushings disease from conditions where ACTH is secreted ectopically. For the Dexamethasone suppression (overnight) test (DST), dexamethasone (Decadron) is given and samples for cortisol levels are collected the following morning. The DST differentiates the causes of increased cortisol levels. For the Metyrapone (overnight) stimulation challenge, metyrapone is given with a snack at midnight. Samples for cortisol, 11-deoxycortisol, and ACTH are collected the following morning. The metyrapone stimulation test distinguishes corticotropin-dependent causes (pituitary Cushing's disease and ectopic Cushing's disease) from corticotropin-independent causes (carcinoma of the lung or thyroid) of increased cortisol levels.

[77610-4] C peptide [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --15 minutes post XXX challenge -- **Component:** C peptide^15M post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** C peptide 15M p chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Measurement of baseline and stimulated C-peptide (after glucagon or a mixed meal challenge) provides a sensitive and clinically validated assessment of beta-cell function for type 1 diabetes patients. [77613-8] Calcitonin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --1 minute post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Calcitonin^1M post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Calcit 1M p chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Calcitonin is a sensitive tumor marker of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC). Patients with MTC have usually very high levels of serum calcitonin that can be used to diagnose the disease. [77615-3] Liver stiffness by US.transient elastography -- **Component:** Liver stiffness **Property:** Pres **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Abdomen>Liver **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.transient elastography  **Short name:** US.TE Liver stiffness  **Definition:** Liver stiffness is measured in a non-invasive manner by vibration-controlled transient elastography (VCTE). VCTE uses an ultrasound transducer mounted on the axis of a mechanical vibrator. The vibrator induces a 50 Hz shear wave through the liver and ultrasounds are used to track the propagation of the shear wave and measure its speed of propagation into the liver. The speed is converted into stiffness using Hooke's law. Stiffness measurements are expressed in kPa. Clinical interpretation of liver stiffness values should always be in the hands of an expert clinician and should be made with full knowledge of patient demographics, disease etiology and essential laboratory parameters.
[77616-1] Liver fibrosis score in Serum or Plasma Calculated by FibroMeter -- **Component:** Liver fibrosis score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.FibroMeter  **Short name:** Liver fibr score SerPl Calc.FibroMeter  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the FibroMeter Virus package of tests, which is intended as a surrogate marker of liver fibrosis, cirrhosis and necro-inflammatory activity in patients with chronic hepatitis C with or without co-infection with HIV and in active carriers of hepatitis B virus. The fibrosis score ranges from 0 to 1 and is calculated using a proprietary algorithm based on seven lab tests (AST, ALT, GGT, prothrombin index, platelet count, urea, and alpha-2-macroglobulin) plus the patient's age and gender.
[77617-9] Toe brachial index - left -- **Component:** Intravascular systolic.digital artery toe left/Brachial artery **Property:** PresRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Peripheral vascular system **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** TBI left  **Definition:** The reading is derived in order to determine the severity of peripheral arterial disease present in a lower extremity. The test is performed using a small blood pressure cuff that is fitted around a toe along with a photoplethysmograph (PPG) infrared light sensor. The resultant toe brachial index (TBI) is generated using the systolic blood pressure readings from the arm and toe.

[77620-3] Arginase-1 [Mass/volume] in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Arginase-1 **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Arginase-1 Ser IA-mCnc  **Definition:** Arginase-1 (s-Arg-1, Liver-type) levels are measured to monitor patients with liver disorders or pre- and postoperative conditions of patients who received partial hepatoectomy with quicker normalization in comparison with aminotransferases (ALT and AST). [77621-1] Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Pleural fluid by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plr fld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** NT-proBNP Plr IA-mCnc  **Definition:** Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) and amino-terminal proBNP (NT-proBNP) are significantly elevated in patients with heart failure because tension or stretching of the cardiac wall, such as occurs with heart failure, stimulates the synthesis of natriuretic peptides. Pleural fluid BNP and NT-proBNP is a useful biomarker for pleural effusions secondary to heart failure.  A pleural NT-proBNP cutoff value of 1,165 pg/mL had a sensitivity of approximately 95% for discriminating heart failure-related effusions from exudates, and a cutoff of 2,300 pg/mL had a sensitivity of approximately 70% for discriminating heart failure-related transudates from non-heart-failure related pleural effusions. [PMID: 22710710] This term was created for, but is not restricted in use to, the Elecsys proBNP II assay, which contains two monoclonal antibodies that recognize epitopes located in the N-terminal part (1-76) of proBNP (1-108).
[77622-9] Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Peritoneal dialysis fluid by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dial fld prt **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** NT-proBNP DiafP IA-mCnc  **Definition:** Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) and amino-terminal proBNP (NT-proBNP) are significantly elevated in patients with heart failure because tension or stretching of the cardiac wall, such as occurs with heart failure, stimulates the synthesis of natriuretic peptides. Elevated levels are associated with adverse outcomes in both hemodialysis (HD) and peritoneal dialysis (PD) patients and are attributable to both ventricular dysfunction and decreased renal clearance. Peritoneal dialysate and plasma NT-proBNP levels are linearly related. [PMID: 21427249] This term was created for, but is not restricted in use to, the Elecsys proBNP II assay, which contains two monoclonal antibodies that recognize epitopes located in the N-terminal part (1-76) of proBNP (1-108).

[77667-4] Calcitonin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --2 minutes post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Calcitonin^2M post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Calcit 2M p chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Calcitonin is a sensitive tumor marker of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC). Patients with MTC have usually very high levels of serum calcitonin that can be used to diagnose the disease. To improve diagnostic sensitivity in family members with small MTCs, or to evaluate postoperative biochemical cure status, measurement of calcitonin stimulated with combined intravenous calcium gluconate and pentagastrin (calcitonin challenge) has been widely adopted. [77685-6] HIV 1 and 2 IgG Ab [Identifier] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoblot -- **Component:** HIV 1 & 2 Ab.IgG **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** IB  **Short name:** HIV 1 & 2 IgG SerPl IB  **Definition:** This assay differentiates between HIV-1 and HIV-2 infections and is intended as a supplementary assay on specimens found to be reactive using an anti-HIV screening procedure.  Five HIV-1 antigens are used in the testing: sgp120 and gp41, which detect HIV-1 specific antibodies, and p31, p24, and p17, which may also cross-react with antibodies to HIV-2. HIV-1 group O peptides are present in the HIV-1 sgp120 band. The antigens gp36 and sgp105 are used to detect HIV-2 antibodies. This term was created for, but not termed in use to, the submitter's INNO-LIA™ HIV I/II Score test kit, which is a line immunoassay, to confirm the presence of antibodies against the human immunodeficiency virus type 1 (HIV-1), including group O, and type 2 (HIV-2) in human serum or plasma.

[7772-7] Wheat triggered histamine release [Units/volume] in Blood -- **Component:** Triticum aestivum basophil bound Ab **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** LHR  **Short name:** Wheat BasoBnd Ab Qn
[77727-6] XXX allergen triggered histamine release [Units/volume] in Blood -- **Component:** XXX allergen basophil bound Ab **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** LHR  **Short name:** XXX allergen BasoBnd Ab Qn  **Definition:** Quantitative determination of histamine release in heparinized whole blood incubated with a suspected allergen. This term is based on, but not limited in use to, IBL International's Histamine-Release assay, which incubates whole blood samples with different concentrations of the suspected allergen. Release of histamine occurs upon stimulation of basophilic granulocytes with amounts depending on their sensibility to the allergen. In the Histamine-Release assay, the percent of histamine release falls within the 100% control (= Total Histamine) and the blank value (= Spontaneous Release). Any positive signal greater than 5% release is regarded as allergen specific positive.

[77742-5] HTLV I and II panel - Serum or Plasma by Immunoblot -- **Component:** HTLV I & II panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** - **Method:** IB  **Short name:** HTLV I+II Pnl SerPl IB  **Definition:** This is a confirmatory panel for the detection and discrimination of antibodies against human T-cell lymphotropic virus type I (HTLV-I) and type II (HTLV-II).  Two gag (p19 I/II, p24 I/II) and two env (gp46 I/II, gp21 I/II) bands are applied as non-type- specific antigens, which confirm the presence of antibodies against HTLV I and II. The type-specific antigens for HTLV-I (gag p19-I, env gp46-I) and for HTLV-II (env gp46-II), are applied to differentiate between HTLV-I and HTLV-II infections. Streptavidin is used as a negative control. This panel was created for the submitter's INNO-LIA HTLV I/II Score test kit.

[77744-1] HTLV I and II Ab band pattern [Interpretation] in Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** HTLV I & II Ab band pattern **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** HTLV I+II Ab Patrn SerPl-Imp  **Definition:** This assay uses antigens that are either recombinant proteins or synthetic peptides  selected to detect  antibodies with a wide specificity to known isolates of the HTLV strains. Type-specific antigens for HTLV-I (gag p19-I, env gp46-I) and for HTLV-II (env gp46-II), distinguish HTLV-I and HTLV-II infections. There are four control lines: one negative control (streptavidin), and three positive control lines. This term was created for the submitter's INNO-LIA HTLV I/II  Score, a Line Immune Assay.

[77889-4] Left ventricular Ejection fraction by US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method -- **Component:** Ejection fraction **Property:** VFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method  **Short name:** LV EF US.M-mode+cube  **Definition:** This term represents the left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) based on left ventricular (LV) end-diastolic and end-systolic volumes as calculated by the Cubed method from measurements taken during M-mode echo. The Teichholz formula is used to calculate left ventricular volume as follows: LV end-diastolic volume = [7/2.4 + Dd]x[Dd]^3, where Dd = the left ventricular internal dimension (short axis) as measured at end-diastole, and LV end-systolic volume = [7/2.4 + Ds]x[Ds]^3, where Ds = the left ventricular internal dimension (short axis) as measured at end-systole. [77892-8] Left ventricular Ejection fraction by US.2D+Calculated by modified Simpson method -- **Component:** Ejection fraction **Property:** VFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D+Calculated by modified Simpson method  **Short name:** LV EF US.2D+mod Simpson  **Definition:** The modified Simpson method is commonly used for volume measurements in 2D cardiac imaging. It uses the biplane method of disks to measure left ventricular volume, meaning that the total volume is calculated by adding together the two-dimensional area measurements of the series of circular or elliptical slices that, taken as a whole, represent the ventricle. [PMID: 16376782] The volume measurements can then be used in calculations of other parameters related to cardiac function, including ejection fraction, cardiac output and cardiac index.

[77897-7] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Body surface area [Mass/Area] by US 2D -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Body surface area **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/BSA 2D US  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. [77898-5] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Body surface area [Mass/Area] by US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Body surface area **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/BSA US.M-mode+cube  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents a BSA-based index. BSA can be calculated by a number of methods including the Mostellar and Du Bois formulas, or in some cases the average adult BSA, 1.73 m2, is used by default. The cube function is a method for calculating left ventricular mass from M-mode echocardiography measurements and assumes a long- to short-axis length ratio of 2:1. The formula is as follows: LV mass = 1.04(IVSd + LVIDd + PWTd)^3 - LVID^3), where IVSd = interventricular septal thickness, LVIDd = left ventricular internal diameter (short axis), and PWTd = posterior wall thickness, all measured during end-diastole. [PMID: 2936235]
[77899-3] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Body surface area [Mass/Area] by US.2D+Calculated by truncated ellipsoid method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Body surface area **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D+Calculated by truncated ellipsoid method  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/BSA US.2D+trunc ellips  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents a BSA-based index. BSA can be calculated by a number of methods including the Mostellar and Du Bois formulas, or in some cases the average adult BSA, 1.73 m2, is used by default. Left ventricular mass calculated by the truncated ellipsoid formula on 2D ultrasound is as follows: 1.05 * Pi * {(b+t)^2 * [2/3 * (a+t)+d-d^3/(3 * (a+t))^2] - b^2 * [2/3 * a + d - d^3/(3 * a^2)]}, where a = distance from the minor axis to the endocardium at the LV apex, b = LV minor radius, t = mean wall thickness, and d = distance from the minor axis to the mitral valve plane. [PMID: 25559473]
[77900-9] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Body surface area [Mass/Area] by US.2D+Calculated by area-length method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Body surface area **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D+Calculated by area-length method  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/BSA US.2D+A-L  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents a BSA-based index. BSA can be calculated by a number of methods including the Mostellar and Du Bois formulas, or in some cases the average adult BSA, 1.73 m2, is used by default. The area-length formula used to calculate LV mass based on measurements taken with 2D echocardiography is as follows: LV mass = 1.055 x 5/6(outer short-axis area x length + 1 cm - inner short-axis area x length). [PMID: 2936235]

[77909-0] Aortic valve Effective regurgitant orifice area by US.doppler+Calculated by PISA method -- **Component:** Effective regurgitant orifice area **Property:** Area **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Aortic valve **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.doppler+Calculated by PISA method  **Short name:** AV EROA PISA  **Definition:** The effective regurgitant orifice area (EROA) is the size of the defect where the valve leaflets are not aligning normally (coaptation defect). The severity of regurgitation is related to the EROA. Proximal isovelocity surface area (PISA) is a method for calculating the EROA using color flow Doppler and is based on flow dynamics and the continuity principle, which states that in the absence of a leak or additional flow into a conduit, the flow rate will be constant along the length of the conduit, whether the conduit is the heart or the regurgitant jet. The EROA is calculated from the flow and peak velocity at the regurgitant orifice, where the flow is based on the radius at the orifice and the Nyquist limit (aliasing velocity), which is the velocity at which the color flow switches from red to blue or blue to red, and the peak velocity is measured using continuous wave Doppler of the jet. [PMID: 17898369]
[77910-8] Aortic valve Effective regurgitant orifice area by US.doppler+Calculated by volumetric method -- **Component:** Effective regurgitant orifice area **Property:** Area **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Aortic valve **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.doppler+Calculated by volumetric method  **Short name:** AV EROA DOP+Calc by vol  **Definition:** The effective regurgitant orifice area (EROA) is the size of the defect where the valve leaflets are not aligning normally (coaptation defect). The severity of regurgitation is related to the EROA. EROA can be calculated by the Volumetric method as regurgitant volume divided by the velocity time integral of the regurgitant jet velocity (VTIRegJet), which is recorded by continuous wave Doppler. [PMID: 18276823]

[77920-7] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 [Mass/Area] by US 2D -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/Ht exp 2.7 2D US  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents an index based on height^2.7.
[77921-5] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 [Mass/Area] by US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.M-mode+Calculated by cube method  **Short name:** LV Myo/Ht exp 2.7 US.M-mode+cube  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents an index based on height^2.7. The cube function is a method for calculating left ventricular mass from M-mode echocardiography measurements and assumes a long- to short-axis length ratio of 2:1. The formula is as follows: LV mass = 1.04(IVSd + LVIDd + PWTd)^3 - LVID^3), where IVSd = interventricular septal thickness, LVIDd = left ventricular internal diameter (short axis), and PWTd = posterior wall thickness, all measured during end-diastole. [PMID: 2936235]
[77922-3] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 [Mass/Area] by US.2D+Calculated by area-length method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D+Calculated by area-length method  **Short name:** LV Myo mass/Ht exp 2.7 ArMass US.2D+A-L  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents an index based on height^2.7. The area-length formula used to calculate LV mass based on measurements taken with 2D echocardiography is as follows: LV mass = 1.055 x 5/6(outer short-axis area x length + 1 cm - inner short-axis area x length). [PMID: 2936235]
[77923-1] Left ventricular Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 [Mass/Area] by US.2D+Calculated by truncated ellipsoid method -- **Component:** Myocardial mass/Height exp 2.7 **Property:** ArMass **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.2D+Calculated by truncated ellipsoid method  **Short name:** LV Myo/Ht exp 2.7 US.2D+trunc ellips  **Definition:** The myocardial index is the left ventricular (LV) mass normalized for body size, which can be approximated by different measures including body surface area (BSA) and height. This term represents an index based on height^2.7. Left ventricular mass calculated by the truncated ellipsoid formula on 2D ultrasound is as follows: 1.05 * Pi * {(b+t)^2 * [2/3 * (a+t)+d-d^3/(3 * (a+t))^2] - b^2 * [2/3 * a + d - d^3/(3 * a^2)]}, where a = distance from the minor axis to the endocardium at the LV apex, b = LV minor radius, t = mean wall thickness, and d = distance from the minor axis to the mitral valve plane. [PMID: 25559473]
[77924-9] Enterobacteriaceae.carbapenem resistant [Presence] in Anorectal or stool specimen by Organism specific culture -- **Component:** Enterobacteriaceae.carbapenem resistant **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Anorectal/Stool **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Organism specific culture  **Short name:** CRE Anorectl/Stl Ql Cult  **Definition:** Carbapenem resistant Enterobacteriacae (CRE) screening may be performed using a rectal swab plated onto a culture medium containing high concentrations of a carbapenem. Any bacterial growth will be identified and full susceptibility performed. Testing can be performed on peri-anal swabs, stool specimen swabs, or stool specimens as well.
[7792-5] Dohle body [Presence] in Blood by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Dohle body **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Dohle Bod Bld Ql Smear
[77925-6] Cortisol [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --10 minutes post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Cortisol^10M post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortis 10M p chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Timed cortisol levels are obtained for adrenocorticotropic hormone challenge tests to assist in diagnosing adrenocortical insufficiency as found in Cushing's syndrome and Addison's disease.  There are different types of challenges. ACTH (adrenocorticotropic hormone) stimulation, where cosyntropin (a synthetic form of ACTH) is given, and then blood is sampled for cortisol levels before the bolus, and at specified times after the bolus. The ACTH stimulation directly evaluates adrenal gland function and indirectly evaluates pituitary gland and hypothalamus function. Cortisol levels rise significantly after administration of cosyntropin if the adrenal glands are functioning normally. For the CRH (corticotropin-releasing hormone) stimulation, CRH is given and cortisol and ACTH levels are determined before the injection, at the injection time, and specified times following injection. The CRH stimulation test is used to distinguish Cushings disease from conditions where ACTH is secreted ectopically.  For the Dexamethasone suppression (overnight) test (DST), dexamethasone (Decadron) is given and samples for cortisol levels are collected the following morning. The DST differentiates the causes of increased cortisol levels. For the Metyrapone (overnight) stimulation challenge, metyrapone is given with a snack at midnight.  Samples for cortisol, 11-deoxycortisol, and ACTH are collected the following morning. The metyrapone stimulation test distinguishes corticotropin-dependent causes (pituitary Cushing's disease and ectopic Cushing's disease) from corticotropin-independent causes (carcinoma of the lung or thyroid) of increased cortisol levels.

[77943-9] Schistosoma mansoni+haematobium+intercalatum [Titer] in Serum by Hemagglutination -- **Component:** Schistosoma mansoni+haematobium+intercalatum **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** HA  **Short name:** S man+haem+interc Titr Ser HA  **Definition:** For detection of Schistosoma mansoni, Schistosoma haematobium, and Schistosoma intercalatum, for patients suffering from Schistosomiasis (Bilharziasi). [77944-7] CD11a cells/cells in Specimen -- **Component:** Cells.CD11a/Cells **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** CD11a Cells NFr Spec  **Definition:** The percent of CD11a positive cells is useful for diagnosis and tracking of treatment for Leukocyte adhesion deficiency (LAD), is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by immunodeficiency resulting in recurrent infections and impaired wound healing. Cells may be gated on lymphocytes, granulocytes or monocytes. Absence of CD11a expression is consistent with LAD type 1. Specimens may include blood, bone marrow, body fluid, or tissue.

[78002-3] CD34 dose in hematopoietic progenitor cell transfusion [#/mass] per recipient body mass -- **Component:** CD34 dose in hematopoietic progenitor cell transfusion **Property:** NCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** CD34 dose in HPC per recipient body mass  **Definition:** Autologous hematopoietic progenitor cell (HPC) transplant is used to treat hematologic malignancies in children, adolescents, and young adults. The dose of CD34+ cells in the transplant, calculated as the number of CD34+ cells infused per kg of the transplant recipient's body weight, is associated with the success of engraftment and patient morbidity and mortality, with higher doses yielding better clinical outcomes. The minimum CD34+ cell dose to achieve fast and effective engraftment is 2-5 × 10^6 cells/kg. [PMID: 26041417]
[78003-1] Nucleated cell dose in hematopoietic progenitor cell transfusion [#/mass] per recipient body mass -- **Component:** Nucleated cell dose in hematopoietic progenitor cell transfusion **Property:** NCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Nuc cell dose HPC per recip body mass  **Definition:** The total nucleated cell count (TNC) in hematopoietic progenitor cells (HPC) is an important predictor of engraftment and clinical outcomes. A TNC of at least 2.5 × 10^7 per kilogram of transplant recipient's body weight is associated with more rapid time to engraftment, decreased risk for graft failure, and improved overall survival. [PMID: 22533817]
[78004-9] Staphylococcus aureus.vancomycin resistance [Susceptibility] Qualitative -- **Component:** Staphylococcus aureus.vancomycin resistance **Property:** Susc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** VRSA Islt Ql  **Definition:** This is a screening assay to determine the sensitivity of Staphylococcus aureus isolates from clinical specimens to vancomycin 6.0 ug/mL. If vancomycin inhibits the growth of the isolate, then that isolate is susceptible to  vancomycin. On the other hand, if an isolate grows despite the presence of vancomycin, it is characterized as vancomycin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (VRSA). [78006-4] Isotopic Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M [Volume Rate/Area] in Urine and Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Glomerular filtration rate **Property:** ArVRat **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine+Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Isotopic based on 1.73 sq M  **Short name:** Isotopic GFR/1.73 sq M Ur+SerPl-ArVRat  **Definition:** This test measures kidney filtration after injection of a radioactive tracer (e.g. technetium 99m DTPA). It is an actual measurement of glomerular filtration rate (GFR). The test is a several hour long kidney function test typically performed to accurately verify the level of renal dysfunction. It requires sequential collection of both blood and urine specimens after the tracer injection. Isotopic GFR may be used for monitoring renal function over time, or in chronic renal failure patients approaching dialysis. [PMID: 1391702] The submitter's lab uses the following formula to determine GFR:    GFR = (UV/P) x (1.73/BSA)     Where, for each timed urine collection,     U = (urine collection CPM = UbkgCPM)   V = urine flow rate: (urine volume / elapsed time) in units of mL/min   P = (average serum or plasma CPM = Bbkg CPM)    The average serum CPM (counts per minute) will be the average of Bo and B1 for the first urine collection (U1) and the average of B1 and B2 for the second collection (U2). Average the calculated GFR results for the two collection periods to obtain the final GFR result.

[78018-9] Toscana virus IgG Ab [Presence] in Cerebral spinal fluid by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Toscana virus Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA  **Short name:** TOSV IgG CSF Ql IA  **Definition:** Because Toscana virus is a known etiologic agent of meningitis, meningoencephalitis, and encephalitis, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) may be tested. Specific antibodies for IgM are present during the acute phase of the Toscana Virus infection, while the presence of specific IgG antibodies in the absence of IgM indicates a previous infection. Cross reactions cannot be excluded with other correlated serotypes of Phlebovirus such as Sicily and Naples. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Diesse ENZYWELL TOSCANA VIRUS IgG/IgM test kit..

[78022-1] Care setting Facility [NHCS] -- **Component:** Care setting **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Facility **Scale:** Nom **Method:** NHCS  **Definition:** The location or setting where a health care service (e.g. procedure, treatment) was provided. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Service Delivery Location template in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: National Health Care Surveys Release 1. The value set associated with this term is based on the National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN) location code system that has been developed over a number of years through CDC's interaction with a variety of healthcare facilities and is intended to serve a variety of reporting needs where coding of healthcare service locations is required.
[78025-4] Outpatient visit discharge disposition [NHCS] -- **Component:** Outpatient visit discharge disposition **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** NHCS  **Definition:** The location and type of care arranged for the patient at the end of an outpatient visit.This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Outpatient Visit Discharge Disposition observation in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: National Health Care Surveys Release 1.
[78026-2] Diagnosis present on admission -- **Component:** Diagnosis present on admission **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Definition:** Indicates whether or not the diagnosis was present on admission (POA) when the patient was admitted into the hospital as an inpatient. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Present on Admission observation in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: National Health Care Surveys Release 1.  Whether or not a specific condition was present at the time a patient was admitted. Note that the concept of "diagnosis" is different than a "condition". Conditions include symptoms, diseases, injuries, and non-pathologic states, while diagnoses are specifically identified by a provider as the source of a patient's signs and symptoms. For example, a patient admitted to the hospital with conditions that include abdominal pain, fever, and vomiting may be diagnosed with appendicitis by a provider based on the history, physical exam, and various test results. To report whether a condition was present on admission, use [LOINC: 89251-3].

[78030-4] Episode of care Type -- **Component:** Type of encounter **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Definition:** Indicates whether a patient's visit is an initial or follow-visit. An episode of care begins with the initial visit for care for a particular problem and ends when the patient is no longer continuing treatment. An initial visit may be diagnostic in nature whereas a follow-up visit may be to check progress or continue therapy. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Episode of Care observation in the HL7 Implementation Guide for CDA® Release 2: National Health Care Surveys Release 1.

[78046-0] Pharmacogenomic analysis basic associated observations panel - Blood or Tissue -- **Component:** Pharmacogenomic analysis basic associated observations panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Pharmacogen basic assoc obs Pnl Bld/T
[78047-8] Cortisol [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --24 hour post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Cortisol^24H post XXX challenge **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortis 24H p XXX chal SerPl-sCnc  **Definition:** Timed cortisol levels are obtained for adrenocorticotropic hormone challenge tests to assist in diagnosing adrenocortical insufficiency as found in Cushing's syndrome and Addison's disease. There are different types of challenges. ACTH (adrenocorticotropic hormone) stimulation, where cosyntropin (a synthetic form of ACTH) is given, and then blood is sampled for cortisol levels before the bolus, and at specified times after the bolus. The ACTH stimulation directly evaluates adrenal gland function and indirectly evaluates pituitary gland and hypothalamus function. Cortisol levels rise significantly after administration of cosyntropin if the adrenal glands are functioning normally. For the CRH (corticotropin-releasing hormone) stimulation, CRH is given and cortisol and ACTH levels are determined before the injection, at the injection time, and specified times following injection. The CRH stimulation test is used to distinguish Cushings disease from conditions where ACTH is secreted ectopically. For the Dexamethasone suppression (overnight) test (DST), dexamethasone (Decadron) is given and samples for cortisol levels are collected the following morning. The DST differentiates the causes of increased cortisol levels. For the Metyrapone (overnight) stimulation challenge, metyrapone is given with a snack at midnight. Samples for cortisol, 11-deoxycortisol, and ACTH are collected the following morning. The metyrapone stimulation test distinguishes corticotropin-dependent causes (pituitary Cushing's disease and ectopic Cushing's disease) from corticotropin-independent causes (carcinoma of the lung or thyroid) of increased cortisol levels.

[78354-8] Arterial-alveolar oxygen Partial pressure ratio -- **Component:** Oxygen.arterial/Oxygen.alveolar **Property:** PPresRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** BldA+Resp.alv **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** a/A PO2 Rto  **Definition:** The arterial/alveolar oxygen tension ratio, also known as a/APO2, is a measure of gas-exchange. While it was originally believed to be less affected by the fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) compared to other measures such as the alveolar-arterial oxygen tension difference (PAO2-PaO2), the a/APO2 has been found to be somewhat dependent on FiO2, especially with higher degrees of pulmonary shunting. [PMID: 3742320]

[78358-9] Acyl CoA dehydrogenases [Enzymatic activity/mass] in Fibroblast by [9,10-3H] palmitate substrate -- **Component:** Acyl CoA dehydrogenases **Property:** CCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Qn **Method:** [9,10-3H] palmitate substrate  **Short name:** ACADs Fib Palmitate-cCnt  **Definition:** Fatty acid oxidation in medium- or long-chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiencies can be measured by quantifying the release of 3H2O from (9,10-3H) palmitate (this term) and myristate [LOINC: 78360-5] substrates added to cultured fibroblast. [PMID: 2109149] Fatty acid (i.e. palmitate or myristate) oxidation rates by fibroblasts are expressed as nanomole of 3H fatty acid (3H FA) oxidized per hour per milligram of cell protein (nmol 3H FA/h/mg protein).[PMID: 12840153]
[78359-7] Acyl CoA dehydrogenases.palmitate substrate/Acyl CoA dehydrogenases.myristate substrate [Enzymatic activity ratio] in Fibroblast -- **Component:** Acyl CoA dehydrogenases.palmitate substrate/Acyl CoA dehydrogenases.myristate substrate **Property:** CRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** ACADs.palmitate/ACADs.myristate Fib-cRto  **Definition:** Medium- and long-chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiencies can be differentiated based on the ratio of palmitate oxidation [LOINC: 78358-9] and myristate oxidation [LOINC: 78360-5] in cultured fibroblasts. Patients with medium chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (MCAD) typically have a high palmitate/myristate (P/M) ratio. Long-chain defects, including VLCAD and LCHAD, generally have a low P/M ratio. [PMID: 10709659]

[78360-5] Acyl CoA dehydrogenases [Enzymatic activity/mass] in Fibroblast by [9,10-3H] myristate substrate -- **Component:** Acyl CoA dehydrogenases **Property:** CCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Qn **Method:** [9,10-3H] myristate substrate  **Short name:** ACADs Fib Myristate-cCnt  **Definition:** One method for diagnosing acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiencies is fatty acid oxidation analysis in cultured fibroblasts. Using multiple fatty acid substrates with varying carbon chain lengths, such as palmitate (16 carbons) [LOINC: 78358-9] and and myristate (14 carbons) (this term), can help differentiate between disorders such as long-chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiency and medium-chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.[PMID: 2109149] Enzyme activity is measured as nanomoles of fatty acid oxidized per hour per milligram of cell protein (nmol FA/h/mg protein). [PMID: 12840153]
[78361-3] Fatty acid and triglyceride panel - 24 hour Stool -- **Component:** Fatty acid & triglyceride panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 24H **System:** Stool **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Fatty acid + triglyceride Pnl 24h Stl  **Definition:** Determination of fatty acids and triglycerides in stool is performed for the diagnosis and monitoring of steatorrhoea. Fat malabsorption occurs in conditions such as celiac disease, chronic pancreatitis, Crohn's disease, and cystic fibrosis. Stool fat is a general concept that includes several components, including triglycerides and fatty acids. Triglycerides levels may be measured separately to determine if the steatorrhoea is caused by pancreatic malabsorption (higher triglycerides to fatty acid ratio) versus intestinal malabsorption (low triglycerides to fatty acid ratio).[PMID: 7774758] The submitter's lab performs an infrared spectroscopy technique to quantitatively measure extracted fatty acids from fecal samples.[PMID: 10817249]

[78369-6] ADHD hyperactive symptoms mean score percent difference [Calculated] -- **Component:** ADHD hyperactive symptoms mean score difference **Property:** PctDiff **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Definition:** This term represents the percent change in the mean ADHD hyperactive symptoms score over time. It was created for, but is not limited in use to, the  NCQA "ADHD: Symptom Reduction in Follow-up Period" eMeasure (eMeasure ID 400). Based on the calculation method as defined in the eMeasure, positive percent change over time indicates a decrease in symptoms, and negative percent change indicates an increase in symptoms. The percent difference is calculated as follows: 1) Subtract the mean hyperactive score at follow-up from the mean score at baseline; 2) Divide the result of the first step by the mean hyperactive score at baseline; and 3) Multiply the result of the second step by 100 to get the percent change.
[78370-4] ADHD diagnostic rating scale interpretation [Vanderbilt ADHD] -- **Component:** ADHD diagnostic rating scale interpretation **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Vanderbilt ADHD  **Definition:** This term represents whether or not a patient meets the criteria for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) based on the Vanderbilt ADHD Diagnostic Rating Scale. Diagnostic criteria include having a positive response (a score of 2 or 3 on an individual item) for at least six core inattentive symptoms (items 1-9) or at least six core hyperactive symptoms (items 10-18), or both. In addition, at least two items in the performance set of items have to have a score of 4, or one item must have a score of 5.
[78371-2] ADHD inattentive symptoms mean score [Calculated] -- **Component:** ADHD inattentive symptoms mean score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Definition:** This term represents the mean inattentive symptoms score at a given point in time based on the Vanderbilt attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) Diagnostic Rating Scale, items 1-9.
[78372-0] ADHD hyperactive symptoms mean score [Calculated] -- **Component:** ADHD hyperactive symptoms mean score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Definition:** This term represents the mean hyperactive symptoms score at a given point in time based on the Vanderbilt attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) Diagnostic Rating Scale, items 10-18.
[78373-8] ADHD inattentive symptoms mean score percent difference [Calculated] -- **Component:** ADHD inattentive symptoms mean score difference **Property:** PctDiff **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Definition:** This term represents the percent change in the mean ADHD inattentive symptoms score over time. It was created for, but is not limited in use to, the NCQA "ADHD: Symptom Reduction in Follow-up Period" eMeasure (eMeasure ID 400). Based on the calculation method as defined in the eMeasure, positive percent change over time indicates a decrease in symptoms, and negative percent change indicates an increase in symptoms. The percent difference is calculated as follows: 1) Subtract the mean inattentive score at follow-up from the mean score at baseline; 2) Divide the result of the first step by the mean inattentive score at baseline; and 3) Multiply the result of the second step by 100 to get the percent change.

[78433-0] Soluble liver IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Line blot -- **Component:** Soluble liver Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Line blot  **Short name:** Soluble liver IgG Ser Ql Line blot  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's test strip, a membrane strip pre-coated with a specific set of antigens in parallel lines. If antibodies to any of those antigens are present in the patient's serum, they will bind to the specific antigen. The bound patient antibodies are then detected using labelled anti-human antibodies. Various manufacturers classify this method as an immunoblot technique. LOINC uses the immunoblot (IB) method to mean that some electrophoretic/chromatographic separation occurs such that position (size) of the band is part of the assessment (as in Western blot). Because this assay does not involve a separation step but rather uses strips with pre-fixed antigens, the LOINC Method is Line blot.

[78512-1] Ophthalmology Summary note -- **Component:** Summary note **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Ophthalmology  **Short name:** Ophthalmol Summary note  **Definition:** An Eye Care Summary document contains a summary of data collected for a patient's eye care summary record. The summary includes advanced directives, allergies, encounters, immunizations, medical equipment, payers, plan of care, social history, vitals, and summary of encounters. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the IHE Eye Care domain Content Profile called "Eye Care Summary Record". The document is a part of the HL7 C-CDA Continuity of Care (CCD) specification. This guide was derived from the IHE Eye Care Technical Committee who is sponsored by the American Academy of Ophthalmology (AAO).
[78513-9] Ophthalmology Summary of encounters note -- **Component:** Summary of encounters note **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Ophthalmology  **Short name:** Ophthalmol Summary encounters  **Definition:** An ocular encounter summary contains information from patient's eye care encounters, such as refractive measurements, lensometry measurements, assessment and plans, and ophthalmic medications. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ocular Encounter Section in the IHE Eye Care domain Content Profile called "Eye Care Summary Record". The document is a super set of the HL7 C-CDA Continuity of Care (CCD) specification. This guide was derived from the IHE Eye Care Technical Committee who is sponsored by the American Academy of Ophthalmology (AAO).
[78514-7] Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A+B+C+w135+Y+Escherichia coli K1 Ag [Presence] in Specimen by Latex agglutination -- **Component:** Neisseria meningitidis serogroups A+B+C+w135+Y+Escherichia coli K1 Ag **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Ord **Method:** LA  **Short name:** N men sg A+B+C+W135+Y+EcK1 Ag Spec Ql LA  **Definition:** This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Directigen (TM) Meningitis Combo Test, a latex agglutination test for the direct qualitative detection of antigens to H. influenzae type B, Streptococcus pneumoniae, Neisseria meningitidis Groups A, B, C, Y or W135 and Escherichia coli K1 in CSF, serum or urine, and Group B streptococcus in CSF and serum.
[78515-4] Lactose [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --15 minutes post dose lactose PO -- **Component:** Lactose^15M post dose lactose PO **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Lactose 15M p Lac PO SerPl-sCnc
[78516-2] Lactose [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --45 minutes post dose lactose PO -- **Component:** Lactose^45M post dose lactose PO **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Lactose 45M p Lac PO SerPl-sCnc
[78517-0] Renin [Mass/volume] in Plasma --2 hours post XXX challenge -- **Component:** Renin^2H post XXX challenge **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Renin 2h p chal Plas-mCnc  **Definition:** The most common substance used in a renin challenge is a weight-dependent dose of captopril, an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor. In a captopril challenge test (CCT), plasma concentration of renin is measured prior to and following the administration of captopril. A significant increase in the post-dose renin level is abnormal and is a marker for renovascular disease. [Wikipedia:Captopril challenge test]
[78518-8] Cholesterol [Moles/volume] in Pericardial fluid -- **Component:** Cholesterol **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Pericard fld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cholest Pcar-sCnc  **Definition:** Measuring cholesterol in pericardial fluid is used to diagnose cholesterol pericarditis, an uncommon form of pericardial disease of unknown pathophysiology, that is characterized by chronic relapsing, usually large, pericardial effusions that are distinctive due to a high level of cholesterol. The condition is usually idiopathic, but can be associated with various systemic diseases such as hypothyroidism, rheumatoid arthritis and tuberculosis, among others. Complications such as cardiac tamponade and chronic constrictive pericarditis are relatively rare. [PMID: 16041969] Cholesterol pericarditis a special type of pericardial effusion rich in LDL-cholesterol, that may form masses along with debris and fibrin, crystallizing over time and depositing in the pericardium, leading to pericarditis. In patients with this form of pericarditis, pericardial LDL-cholesterol levels are higher than those in the plasma, while in healthy individuals, they are lower.

[78529-5] Adenovirus Ag [Presence] in Stool by Rapid immunoassay -- **Component:** Adenovirus Ag **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stool **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA.rapid  **Short name:** HAdV Ag Stl Ql IA.rapid  **Definition:** This test detects antibodies against the adenovirus hexon antigen present in all adenovirus subtypes. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's Operon Stick Rota-Adeno / Simple Rota-Adeno in feces test kit. Testing can be performed in approximately 20 minutes.

[78532-9] Creatinine [Moles/volume] in Peritoneal dialysis fluid --2 hour dwell specimen -- **Component:** Creatinine^2H dwell specimen **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dial fld prt **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Creat 2H dwell spec DiafP-sCnc  **Definition:** A Peritoneal Equilibration Test (PET) is used to assess transport properties of the peritoneal membrane in patients  undergoing peritoneal dialysis. Patients arrive at the dialysis unit in the morning with their overnight dwell fluid still in the abdomen. The overnight fluid is drained, and after complete drainage, in the classic PET, 2000 ml of a 2.27% glucose solution is instilled and peritoneal dialysis is begun. Ten ml samples are drawn at time 0, 2, and 4 hours to measure creatinine, urea, and glucose. The PET assesses the rate of solute equilibration between peritoneal capillary blood and dialysate based on the ratio of the solute concentrations in dialysate and plasma (D/P). PET results are used to adjust various aspects of a patient's peritoneal dialysis regimen, including dwell time, number of exchanges, and dialysis volume and composition.  [PMID: 7474677]

[78685-5] Platelet aggregation [Units/volume] in Blood by collagen induced -- **Component:** Platelet aggregation **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Aggregometry.collagen induced  **Short name:** PA Bld Coll-aCnc  **Definition:** Assay for the quantitative in vitro determination of platelet function triggered by collagen. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Cobas COLtest, a collagen induced platelet aggregation assay on whole blood samples by impedance aggregometry method.
[78686-3] Platelet aggregation [Units/volume] in Blood by ristocetin induced High dose -- **Component:** Platelet aggregation **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Aggregometry.ristocetin induced.high dose  **Short name:** PA Bld Rist hi dose-aCnc  **Definition:** Assay for the quantitative in vitro determination of platelet function triggered by a high concentration of ristocetin. In the presence of ristocetin, platelets bind to von Willebrand Factor (VWF) by means of glycoprotein Ib (GPIb) receptors and triggers platelet activation and aggregation. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Cobas RISTOtest, a quantitative in vitro assay using a high concentration of ristocetin (0.77 mg/mL) to stimulate strong platelet aggregation in whole blood samples by impedance aggregometry.
[78687-1] Platelet aggregation [Units/volume] in Blood by thrombin receptor activating peptide-6 induced -- **Component:** Platelet aggregation **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Aggregometry.thrombin receptor activating peptide-6 induced  **Short name:** PA Bld TRAP-6 induced-aCnc  **Definition:** Assay for the quantitative in vitro determination of platelet function triggered by thrombin receptor activating peptide-6 (TRAP-6), a potent platelet activator that stimulates platelet aggregation via the thrombin receptor PAR-1. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Cobas TRAPtest, a platelet function assay performed on whole blood samples by impedance aggregometry method.
[78688-9] Platelet aggregation [Units/volume] in Blood by adenosine diphosphate+prostaglandin E1 induced -- **Component:** Platelet aggregation **Property:** ACnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Aggregometry.adenosine diphosphate+prostaglandin E1 induced  **Short name:** PA Bld ADP+PGE1 induced-aCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative in vitro determination of platelet function in whole blood triggered by the addition of adenosine diphosphate (ADP) and prostaglandin E1 (PGE1). ADP induces platelet aggregation by two pathways mediated by the platelet receptors P2Y1 and P2Y12, respectively. The addition of PGE1 inhibits the P2Y1 activation pathway, which enables more specific evaluation of substances, such as clopidogrel, that affect platelet aggregation through the P2Y12 pathway. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Cobas Prostaglandin E1 Reagent used in conjunction with the ADPtest. The combination of the two is also known as ADPtest High Sensitivity (HS) assay.

In the presence of ristocetin, platelets bind to von Willebrand Factor (VWF) by means of glycoprotein Ib (GPIb) receptors and triggers platelet activation and aggregation. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Cobas RISTOtest, a quantitative in vitro assay using a high concentration of ristocetin (0.77 mg/mL) to stimulate strong platelet aggregation in whole blood samples by impedance aggregometry.

[78746-5] Country of birth [Location] -- **Component:** Country of birth **Property:** Loc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Country of birth
[78747-3] Peritoneal equilibration test panel - Peritoneal dialysis fluid -- **Component:** Peritoneal equilibration test panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dial fld prt **Scale:** -  **Short name:** PET Pnl DiafP  **Definition:** This panel contains terms for the Peritoneal Equilibration Test (PET) which is used to assess transport properties of the peritoneal membrane for patients undergoing peritoneal dialysis. Patients arrive at the dialysis unit in the morning with their overnight dwell fluid still in the abdomen. The overnight fluid is drained, and after complete drainage, in the classic PET, 2000 ml of a 2.27% glucose solution is instilled and peritoneal dialysis is begun. Ten ml samples are drawn at time 0, 2, and 4 hours to measure creatinine, urea, and glucose. The PET assesses the rate of solute equilibration between peritoneal capillary blood and dialysate based on the ratio of the solute concentrations in dialysate and plasma (D/P). PET results are used to adjust various aspects of a patient's peritoneal dialysis regimen, including dwell time, number of exchanges, and dialysis volume and composition.  [PMID: 7474677]

Capillary electrophoresis has a much higher resolution compared to other electrophoresis methods.

[78761-4] 7-Aminoclonazepam [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** 7-Aminoclonazepam **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** 7Aminoclonazepam Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** 7-aminoclonazepam is deposited in hair in much higher quantities than the parent drug clonazepam, and remains there for extended periods of time. One study indicates that, assuming the average hair growth of 0.8-1.3 cm per month, the presence of 7-aminoclonazepam in distal hair segments could represent over a 1.5 year history of clonazepam use.  It is possible to detect 7-aminoclonazepam in hair after a single dose of clonazepam. [PMID: 11043668]
[78762-2] 7-Aminoflunitrazepam [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** 7-Aminoflunitrazepam **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** 7Aminoflunitrazepam Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** Hair testing is widely employed to evaluate the use of benzodiazepine drugs of abuse, such as Flunitrazepam  and its metabolite 7-Aminoflunitrazepam.  Hair analysis may be used to monitor a single dose or to document exposure in forensic cases involving drug-facilitated crimes. [PMID: 16154525]
[78763-0] OPC3373 (Metabolite of Ariprazole) [Mass/volume] in Urine -- adjusted for lean body mass+urine creatinine -- **Component:** OPC3373^^adjusted for lean body mass+urine creatinine **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** OPC3373 adj bodymass+crea Ur-mCnc  **Definition:** This term represents the patient's drug level normalized for urine creatinine and lean body mass. It was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ameritox Medication Monitoring system, which assesses a patient's medication compliance by comparing the patient's result to a reference database of results from chronic pain patients who were known to be adherent to their medication regimen. In order to compare to the reference database, the patient's result is first normalized based on urine creatinine and lean body mass using a proprietary algorithm, and then the normalized result is converted to a Z-score based on the reference population.
