[78771-3] 9-Hydroxyrisperidone [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** 9-Hydroxyrisperidone **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** 9OH-risperidone Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** In general hair analysis provides a longer surveillance window for drug use than other methods because hair retains drugs longer (weeks or months, compared with days detectable in blood or urine).  Collecting hair specimens  is also less invasive than urine or blood sampling and provides a simple and fast method for detection and quantification of Risperidone (RSP) and its major metabolite, 9-hydroxyrisperidone (9-OH-RSP). One study suggests a better relationship between the administered dose and hair concentration for 9-OH-RSP, than for the parent drug. [PMID: 19595645] Drawbacks for hair analysis include expense, and environmental contamination of the specimen.
[78772-1] Abacavir [Mass/volume] in Amniotic fluid -- **Component:** Abacavir **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Amnio fld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Abacavir Amn-mCnc  **Definition:** Abacavir, an antiretroviral given to human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)-infected pregnant women, crosses the placenta by simple diffusion and concentrates in the amniotic fluid, probably through fetal urinary excretion. Antiretrovirals in the amniotic fluid at delivery may amount to an oral loading dose for the newborn, which would continue to be absorbed in the hours following birth. Further investigations are needed to determine the long-term effect of antiretroviral medication exposure through amniotic fluid. [PMID: 15504861]

[78800-0] Atropine [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** Atropine **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Atropine Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** Datura stramonium is an herbaceous annual plant that contains tropane alkaloids such as atropine and scopolamine. Leaves and seeds of D. stramonium appear to be rich in atropine contrary to D. inoxia which has a higher dosage of scopolamine than atropine. Exposures to Datura appear to be minor most of the time leading to a complete recovery, however there have been fatal cases. In the most serious cases, depression of central nervous system with convulsive coma and risk of apnea have been observed. Hair analysis for Datura spp. intoxication can be performed when neither blood nor urine specimens have been collected.  LC-MS/MS  appears to be the most efficient method when searching for very low concentrations of tropane alkaloids. [PMID: 23084788]

[78805-9] Barium [Moles/mass] in Tissue -- **Component:** Barium **Property:** SCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Barium Tiss-sCnt  **Definition:** Barium can be measured in body tissues and fluids, such as bones, blood, serum, urine, and feces. Testing is usually done only for cases of severe barium poisoning and for medical research. Barium accumulates in bone.  Inhaled barium can be absorbed through the lung or directly from the nasal membrane into the bloodstream.  Insoluble compounds, such as barium sulfate, accumulate in the lungs and are cleared slowly by ciliary action. Barium is eliminated in the urine and in the feces, the rates varying with the route of administration.

Pleural fluid, bile, etc. may be used for medico-legal testing when blood or urine is not available.

[78828-1] fentaNYL [Z-score] in Urine -- **Component:** fentaNYL **Property:** Zscore **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** fentaNYL Z-score Ur  **Definition:** This term represents the patient's drug level normalized for urine creatinine and lean body mass. It was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ameritox Medication Monitoring system, which assesses a patient's medication compliance by comparing the patient's result to a reference database of results from chronic pain patients who were known to be adherent to their medication regimen. In order to compare to the reference database, the patient's result is first normalized based on urine creatinine and lean body mass using a proprietary algorithm, and then the normalized result is converted to a Z-score based on the reference population.
[78829-9] Gamma hydroxybutyrate [Mass/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** Gamma hydroxybutyrate **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** G-OH-Butyr Hair-mCnt  **Definition:** Gamma hydroxybutyrate can be detected in hair even months after ingestion. To detect gamma hydroxybutyrate in urine, the sample must be taken within 8-12 hours of  ingestion. [PMID: 24350046]
[78830-7] HYDROcodone [Z-score] in Urine -- **Component:** HYDROcodone **Property:** Zscore **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** HYDROcodone Z-score Ur  **Definition:** This term represents the Z-score for a patient's drug result. It was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ameritox Medication Monitoring system, which assesses a patient's medication compliance by comparing the patient's result to a reference database of results from chronic pain patients who were known to be adherent to their medication regimen. In order to compare to the reference database, the patient's result is first normalized based on urine creatinine and lean body mass using a proprietary algorithm, and then the normalized result is converted to a Z-score based on the reference population. If the patient's Z-score is between -2 and +2, the patient is likely to be taking the drug as prescribed. If the score is less than -2, the patient's drug level is lower than expected, which could be a sign that the patient stopped taking the drug or has altered metabolism. If the score is greater than +2, the patient's drug level is higher than expected, which could occur for a number of reasons, including overuse of the drug or impaired metabolism due to liver or kidney disease.

[78872-9] OPC3373 (Metabolite of Ariprazole) [Z-score] in Urine -- **Component:** OPC3373 **Property:** Zscore **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** OPC3373 Z-score Ur  **Definition:** This term represents the Z-score for a patient's drug result. It was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ameritox Medication Monitoring system, which assesses a patient's medication compliance by comparing the patient's result to a reference database of results from chronic pain patients who were known to be adherent to their medication regimen. In order to compare to the reference database, the patient's result is first normalized based on urine creatinine and lean body mass using a proprietary algorithm, and then the normalized result is converted to a Z-score based on the reference population. If the patient's Z-score is between -2 and +2, the patient is likely to be taking the drug as prescribed. If the score is less than -2, the patient's drug level is lower than expected, which could be a sign that the patient stopped taking the drug or has altered metabolism. If the score is greater than +2, the patient's drug level is higher than expected, which could occur for a number of reasons, including overuse of the drug or impaired metabolism due to liver or kidney disease.

If the score is greater than +2, the patient's drug level is higher than expected, which could occur for a number of reasons, including overuse of the drug or impaired metabolism due to liver or kidney disease.

[78891-9] Tricyclic antidepressants [Presence] in Hair -- **Component:** Tricyclic antidepressants **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Tricyclics Hair Ql  **Definition:** Hair analysis provides a longer surveillance window for drug use than other methods because hair retains drugs longer (weeks or months, compared with days detectable in blood or urine).  Collecting hair specimens also is less invasive than urine or blood sampling. Drawbacks for hair analysis include expense, and environmental contamination of the specimen. [78892-7] Silver [Moles/mass] in Tissue -- **Component:** Silver **Property:** SCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Silver Tiss-sCnt  **Definition:** Normally concentrations of silver in human tissues are low. Overexposure can cause silver to accumulate in the skin, liver, kidneys, corneas, gingiva, mucous membranes, nails and spleen. The adverse effects of chronic exposure to silver are a permanent bluish-gray discoloration of the skin (argyria) or eyes (argyrosis). Besides argyria and argyrosis, exposure to soluble silver compounds may produce other toxic effects, including liver and kidney damage, irritation of the eyes, skin, respiratory, and intestinal tract, and changes in blood cells.[PMID: 15964881]

[78894-3] Liver fibrosis score in Serum or Plasma Calculated by HepaScore -- **Component:** Liver fibrosis score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.HepaScore  **Short name:** Liver fibr score SerPl Calc HepaScore  **Definition:** This term was created for the HepaScore liver fibrosis test, which is a marker of liver fibrosis in patients with hepatitis C infection. The score ranges from 0 to 1 and is calculated based on four lab tests (GGT, bilirubin, alpha-2-macroglobulin, and hyaluronic acid) plus the patient's age and gender. The HepaScore is reported with a central cut-off of 0.50, with a value ≥ 0.50 indicating that significant liver fibrosis is present (positive predictive value 88%) and a value < 0.50 indicating that advanced fibrosis is absent (negative predictive value 95%) for patients with hepatitis C infection. The HepaScore logistic regression model is as follows: y = exp[-4.185818 - (0.0249 x age) + (0.7464 x sex) + (1.0039 x α2-macroglobulin) + (0.0302 x hyaluronic acid) + (0.0691 x bilirubin) - (0.0012 x GGT)] with age provided in years, male sex = 1, female sex = 0, α2-macroglobulin in g/L, hyaluronate in μg/L, bilirubin in μmol/L, and GGT in U/L. The score itself is calculated as y/(1 +y). [PMID: 16055434]

Information about the number of vessels and specific vessels involved helps guide patient management decisions (e.g., whether to treat with diet and exercise, medication alone, percutaneous coronary intervention or coronary artery bypass graft), and the extent of disease is associated with risk of other conditions such as myocardial infarction.

[78918-0] 7-Hydroxyquetiapine [Z-score] in Urine -- **Component:** 7-Hydroxyquetiapine **Property:** Zscore **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** 7OH-quetiapine Z-score Ur  **Definition:** This term represents the Z-score for a patient's drug result. It was created for, but not limited in use to, the Ameritox Medication Monitoring system, which assesses a patient's medication compliance by comparing the patient's result to a reference database of results from chronic pain patients who were known to be adherent to their medication regimen. In order to compare to the reference database, the patient's result is first normalized based on urine creatinine and lean body mass using a proprietary algorithm, and then the normalized result is converted to a Z-score based on the reference population. If the patient's Z-score is between -2 and +2, the patient is likely to be taking the drug as prescribed. If the score is less than -2, the patient's drug level is lower than expected, which could be a sign that the patient stopped taking the drug or has altered metabolism. If the score is greater than +2, the patient's drug level is higher than expected, which could occur for a number of reasons, including overuse of the drug or impaired metabolism due to liver or kidney disease.

For example, a patient might have a visual impairment that could be supported by observations of visual acuity, keratometry, or refraction.  Some other examples are coded values for vestibular functions like sense of position, balance, and proprioception, temperature, vibration, and pressure. [78921-4] Bordetella pertussis and Bordetella parapertussis DNA and culture panel - Nasopharynx -- **Component:** Bordetella pertussis & Bordetella parapertussis panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Nph **Scale:** -  **Short name:** B pert+parapert DNA+cult Pnl Nph  **Definition:** Pertussis is an acute infectious disease caused by the bacterium Bordetella pertussis. PCR has increased sensitivity and faster results reporting, however culture is still considered the gold standard laboratory test and the most specific laboratory test for pertussis.  PCR is done to detect the presence of a repetitive insertion sequence IS481 that has been specifically found to be present in up to 100 copies within the genome of B. pertussis.  8-10 copies of this insertion sequence can also be found in B. holmesii however. On rare occasion, a copy of this sequence is also found in B. bronchiseptica, thus these two species may test positive for the PCR assay.  Concurrent culturing is used to differentiate these two species from B. pertussis and confirm the organism, while PCR allows preliminary results to be reported the same day that the specimen is received.

[78950-3] Left ventricle Segmental wall motion panel by Angiogram -- **Component:** Segmental wall motion panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** - **Method:** Angiogram  **Short name:** LV SWM Pnl Angiogram  **Definition:** The American Heart Association (AHA) recommends assessing left ventricular wall motion by angiography using a seven segment model with right and left anterior oblique views, compared to the 17 segment model the AHA recommends for assessing segmental wall motion by echocardiography. [78960-2] Aldosterone and renin concentration panel - Plasma -- **Component:** Aldosterone & renin concentration panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Aldosterone + ARC + ARR Pnl Plas  **Definition:** The aldosterone to renin ratio (ARR) can be determined by dividing the molar concentration of aldosterone [LOINC: 14586-2] divided by the mass concentration of renin (also known as active renin concentration or 'ARC') [LOINC: 35570-1]. ARR results above 130 pmol/ng indicate primary aldosteronism. ARR results between 100 - 130 pmol/ng are indeterminate and suggest a need for repeat testing.[PMID: 21912488] The ARR can also be determined by dividing the mass concentration of aldosterone by the renin activity in blood plasma [LOINC: 55151-5].[PMID: 15483077]
[78961-0] IgG clearance/Albumin clearance [Ratio] in Urine and Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** IgG clearance/Albumin clearance **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine+Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** IgG/Alb clear Ur+SerPl-Rto  **Definition:** The ratio of IgG to albumin clearances, the selectivity index, is useful in the diagnosis and management of proteinuria. Both IgG and albumin levels are measured in serum and urine and the selectivity index (SI) is determined where SI = (uIgG/sIgG)/(uAlb/sAlb) or (uIgG/sIgG * sAlb/uAlb).[PMID: 20921302]
[78962-8] IgG clearance/Transferrin clearance [Ratio] in Urine and Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** IgG clearance/Transferrin clearance **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine+Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** IgG/Transf clear Ur+SerPl-Rto  **Definition:** The ratio of IgG to transferrin clearances, the selectivity index, is useful in the diagnosis and management of proteinuria. Both IgG and transferrin levels are measured in serum and urine and the selectivity index (SI) is calculated according to the formula of Cameron and Blandford where SI = (uIgG/sIgG)/(uTf/sTf) or (uIgG/sIgG * sTf/uTf).[PMID: 4161419] An index of below 0.1 is indicative of highly selective proteinuria.[PMID: 11012907]

[78973-5] CT scanogram Lower extremity - bilateral for leg measurement WO contrast -- **Component:** Multisection for leg measurement^WO contrast **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Lower extremity.bilateral **Scale:** Doc **Method:** CT.scanogram  **Short name:** CT.scanogram LE-Bl leg meas WO contr  **Definition:** A computed tomography (CT) scanogram is a low-dose, non-contrast radiation imaging modality used for a variety of indications, including ante version, femur length and tibial length. A CT scanogram can be used for leg measurement when there are flexion contractures that would distort anteroposterior images, and when lateral images from a CT can provide bone leg measurement in patients with leg inequalities (e.g., ununited fracture). CT measurements of anteversion are based on the angle of rotation of the femoral neck relative to the femoral condyles and are used to determine whether there are rotational components, not simply a discrepancy in leg length.

[78988-3] Cortisol [Moles/mass] in Hair -- **Component:** Cortisol **Property:** SCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortis Hair-sCnt  **Definition:** Elevated cortisol levels are associated with Cushing Syndrome (CS), and the severity of CS is associated with the duration and extent of excess glucocorticoid exposure. Measuring the cortisol level in hair can provide information about cortisol levels up to 18 months before the time of sampling, while serum, saliva and urine samples reflect only current systemic cortisol levels. Hair cortisol concentrations vary in accordance with the clinical course and may provide a way to assess cortisol exposure over time. [PMID: 19609841]

[78992-5] Melatonin [Mass/volume] in Saliva (oral fluid) -- **Component:** Melatonin **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Saliva **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Melatonin Sal-mCnc  **Definition:** Melatonin assays are used to evaluate the phase position of the biological clock and alignment of an individual's circadian rhythm with a typical 24-hour sleep-wake cycle. Saliva melatonin levels are relatively unaffected by changes in posture and at any given time, the saliva concentration is approximately 30% of that in the plasma. Given that saliva sampling is noninvasive and painless, saliva provides a convenient alternative to plasma. Timed melatonin measurement is used to identify sleep-wake cycle disorders, and melatonin can also be used to determine pineal gland function and help in the diagnosis of pineal tumors. [PMID: 16761382]
[78993-3] Prolactin isoforms panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Prolactin isoforms panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Prolactin isoforms Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** The prolactin isoforms panel includes individual tests for monomeric, dimeric, and macroprolactin, as well as fraction terms for each isomer and an interpretation based on all of the results. Monomeric prolactin is the most common form of circulating prolactin. The dimeric form ("big-prolactin") has a molecular weight of ~50 kD, and polymeric form ("big-big prolactin") has a molecular weight of ~ 150 kD). Big-big prolactin, known as macroprolactin, and big prolactin are formed in the circulation after secretion of prolactin from the pituitary. They have a longer half-life than monomeric prolactin and are not biologically active. However, typical prolactin assays do not differentiate between the different forms of prolactin, and the presence of big and big-big prolactin can lead to a false diagnosis of hyperprolactinemia. Methods such as PEG precipitation can separate macroprolactin and big prolactin from monomeric prolactin in order to obtain a more accurate measurement of biologically active prolactin, and chromatography can be used to further characterize the isoforms if necessary. [PMCID: PMC3719302]

[79072-5] CT Guidance for radiation treatment of Unspecified body region -- **Component:** Guidance for radiation treatment **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Doc **Method:** CT  **Short name:** CT Guided RT  **Definition:** One of the first steps in radiation oncology treatment planning is radiation simulation, during which detailed imaging is done to show the precise location of a patient's tumor and the surrounding healthy tissue. This information guides treatment planning in that it is used to determine the optimal patient positioning, to mark the patient's skin with small dots of permanent ink (tattoos) to ensure that the same precise area is treated each time, and to create custom immobilization devices such as a face mask or a leg mold. Various modalities may be used for radiation simulation to guide radiation treatment planning, including computed tomography (CT) (this term) or CT WO & W contrast [LOINC: 92926-5], magnetic resonance imaging (MR) [LOINC: 82123-1], positron emission tomography (PT) [LOINC: 82126-4] and ultrasound (US) [LOINC: 82124-9].

[79102-0] Heart transplant acute cellular rejection risk [Score] in Mononuclear cells Calculated by AlloMap -- **Component:** Heart transplant acute cellular rejection risk **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld MC **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.AlloMap  **Short name:** Heart tx acute reject Score MC AlloMap  **Definition:** This term is used to report the risk score for moderate to severe (grade 3A or higher) acute cellular transplant rejection in heart transplant recipients with stable allograft function. The test assesses the expression of 11 genes (ITGAM, FLT3, IL1R2, G6B, PF4, WDR40A, MIR, ARHU, PDCD1, ITGA4 AND SEMA7A) using PCR with RNA isolated from peripheral blood mononuclear cells, and the gene expression data is then used to calculate a score that ranges from 0-40, with a lower score indicating a lower risk for rejection. In the Cardiac Allograft Rejection Gene Expression Observational (CARGO) study, patients who were at least 12 months post-transplant and who had a score less than 30 were very unlikely to have grade 3A acute cellular rejection at the time of testing (negative predictive value 99.6%). [PMID: 16433769] This term was created for XDx Reference Laboratory's AlloMap test, which is indicated for heart transplant patients who are at least 15 years of age and 55 or more days post-transplant.

[79114-5] Total score - Patient examination [GPCOG] -- **Component:** Total score - Patient examination **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** GPCOG  **Definition:** The total score for the GPCOG Patient Examination is equal to the total number of correct answers out of 9 questions. If patient scores 9, no significant cognitive impairment and further testing not necessary. If patient scores 5-8, more information required. Proceed with Step 2, informant section. If patient scores 0-4, cognitive impairment is indicated. Conduct standard investigations.
[79115-2] General Practitioner Assessment of Cognition [GPCOG] -- **Component:** General Practitioner Assessment of Cognition **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** GPCOG  **Definition:** The General Practitioner assessment of Cognition (GPCOG) is a screening tool for cognitive impairment and designed for primary care settings.[PMID: 11943052] The GPCOG consists of two parts: a cognitive assessment conducted with the patient and an informant questionnaire, which is only performed if the results of the cognitive assessment are inconclusive, i.e. patient scores between 5-8 out of 9. If the patient scores between 0-4 on the cognitive assessment, then cognitive impairment is indicated. If patient scores 9 (all answers are correct), no significant cognitive impairment is observed and further testing not necessary. For the informant questionnaire, if the patient scores 0-3 out of 6, cognitive impairment is indicated.
[79116-0] Informant interview panel [GPCOG] -- **Component:** Informant interview panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** GPCOG  **Definition:** The Informant Interview section of the General Practitioner Assessment of Cognition (GPCOG) screening test [LOINC: 79115-2] is performed when the patient scores between 5-8 out of 9 on the patient GPCOG cognitive assessment [LOINC: 79117-8 ]. To determine if the patient has cognitive impairment, the patient's informant is asked six questions about how the patient is compared to when s/he was well (e.g. 5 - 10 years ago). The total score is equal to the number of items answered 'no', 'don't know' or 'N/A'. If the patient scores between 0-3 out of 6, cognitive impairment is indicated.
[79117-8] Patient examination panel [GPCOG] -- **Component:** Patient examination panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** GPCOG  **Definition:** The Patient Examination portion of the General Practitioner assessment of Cognition (GPCOG) is a cognitive assessment for recall, time orientation, clock drawing, and ability to provide current news information.[PMID: 11943052] The screening tool is designed for primary care settings. Scores between 0-9 where 9 indicates no significant cognitive impairment (all answers are correct), 5-8 is equivocal, and 0-4 indicates cognitive impairment. If the patient scores 5-8, the patient's informant completes the informant questionnaire [LOINC: 79116-0]. If the patient scores 0-3 out of 6 on the informant questionnaire, cognitive impairment is indicated.

[79164-0] CES1 gene c.428G>A [Presence] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** CES1 gene.c.428G>A **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CES1 c.428G>A Bld/T Ql  **Definition:** The CES1 gene encodes carboxylesterase 1A1 (CES1A1), which is the key enzyme involved in the first-pass metabolism of methylphenidate (Ritalin). [79192-1] Maternal pregnancy information Narrative -- **Component:** Pregnancy information **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Mother **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Maternal pregnancy info  **Definition:** The maternal information section contains clinical history information about the labor and delivery (e.g. method of delivery, procedures, pregnancy outcome, maternal morbidity, medications, etc.), social history (e.g. alcoholic drinks per week) and nutritional status (e.g. nutrition assessment findings, special diet requirements and restrictions) from when the patient's mother was pregnant with patient. [79193-9] Glucose [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma --15 minutes post dose arginine+insulin -- **Component:** Glucose^15M post dose arginine+insulin **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Glucose 15M p Arg+Ins SerPl-mCnc  **Definition:** This test is part of one growth hormone deficiency testing procedure, in which glucose and growth hormone levels are measured at specific time intervals after the administration of arginine and insulin. Normally, arginine and insulin-induced hypoglycemia stimulate the release of growth hormone, so patients without growth hormone deficiency will have a significant increase in serum growth hormone in response to arginine and insulin administration. In patients with growth hormone deficiency, however, the increase in and the absolute peak of the growth hormone level will be much lower than expected. Glucose levels are monitored during this procedure because a certain level of hypoglycemia has to be achieved in order to stimulate growth hormone release, and periodic glucose measurements can be used to titrate the insulin dose as needed.

Normally, arginine and insulin-induced hypoglycemia stimulate the release of growth hormone, so patients without growth hormone deficiency will have a significant increase in serum growth hormone in response to arginine and insulin administration. In patients with growth hormone deficiency, however, the increase in and the absolute peak of the growth hormone level will be much lower than expected.

[79263-0] Creatinine [Moles/volume] in Peritoneal dialysis fluid --overnight dwell -- **Component:** Creatinine^overnight dwell **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dial fld prt **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Creat Ovrngt Dwell DiafP-sCnc  **Definition:** A Peritoneal Equilibration Test (PET) is used to assess transport properties of the peritoneal membrane in patients undergoing peritoneal dialysis. Patients arrive at the dialysis unit in the morning with their overnight dwell fluid still in the abdomen. The overnight fluid is completely drained and sampled for testing. In the classic PET, 2000 ml of a 2.27% glucose solution is instilled and peritoneal dialysis is begun. Ten ml samples are drawn at time 0, 2, and 4 hours to measure creatinine, urea, and glucose. The PET assesses the rate of solute equilibration between peritoneal capillary blood and dialysate based on the ratio of the solute concentrations in dialysate and plasma (D/P). PET results are used to adjust various aspects of a patient's peritoneal dialysis regimen, including dwell time, number of exchanges, and dialysis volume and composition. [PMID: 7474677]

[79266-3] Lead [Mass/mass] in Dentin -- **Component:** Lead **Property:** MCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Dentin **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Lead Dentin-mCnt  **Definition:** The concentration of lead (Pb) in teeth can be used as an index of environmental pollution. [79288-7] Creatine and guanidinoacetate panel - Cerebral spinal fluid -- **Component:** Creatine & guanidinoacetate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cr and GAA Pnl CSF  **Definition:** Measurements of guanidinoacetate (GAA) and creatine (Cr) in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) are performed for biochemical diagnosis of Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency, an inherited metabolic disorder of creatine metabolism that is characterized by mental retardation, developmental delay or regression, mental retardation, expressive and cognitive speech delay, and intractable seizures and movement disturbances.[OMIM: 612736] [PMID: 15234334]

[79291-1] Creatine and guanidinoacetate pattern [Interpretation] in Serum or Plasma Narrative -- **Component:** Creatine & guanidinoacetate pattern **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Cr and GAA pattern SerPl-Imp  **Definition:** Increased guanidinoacetate (GAA) is a marker for the diagnosis of guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency. Almeidaa et al (2004) found the GAA levels in plasma to be 4-22 times higher in patients with GAMT deficiency compared to controls.[PMID: 15234334]

Almeidaa et al (2004) found the GAA levels to be 4-130 times increased in urine in patients with GAMT deficiency compared to controls.[PMID: 15234334]

[79294-5] D- and L-2-hydroxyglutarate panel - Cerebral spinal fluid -- **Component:** D- & L-2-hydroxyglutarate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** -  **Short name:** D2HG and L2HG Pnl CSF  **Definition:** This panel contains results for D-2-hydroxyglutarate (D-2-HG) and L-2-hydroxyglutarate (L-2-HG), the isomeric forms of 2-hydroxyglutaric acid. Accumulation of D-2-HG and/or L-2-HG in body fluids (e.g. urine, CSF, amniotic fluid, plasma) are the biochemical hallmarks of 2-hydroxyglutaric acidurias (2-HGAs), inborn organic acidurias that cause neurological impairment at a young age.[PMID: 22391998] Results of this panel are used to differentiate D-2-HGA, L-2-HGA and D,L-2-HGA.

[79333-1] Viscosity of Cervical mucus Qualitative -- **Component:** Viscosity **Property:** Visc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Cvm **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Visc Cerv mucus Ql  **Definition:** Cervical mucus is continuously produced by the secretory cells of the endocervix, and its quality and quantity vary depending upon the hormonal status of the estrous cycle. The viscosity of the cervical mucus is one of the factors which determine the ability of spermatozoa to penetrate into the uterine cavity. Viscosity is the measurement of resistance of a liquid to flow and can be measured using qualitative or quantitative methods.

[79343-0] Dimethylacetals pattern [Interpretation] in Red Blood Cells Narrative -- **Component:** Dimethylacetals pattern **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** RBC **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** DMA pattern RBC-Imp  **Definition:** The pattern of dimethylacetals (DMAs), including DMA 16:0 (1,1-Dimethoxyhexadecane), DMA 18:0 (1,1-Dimethoxyoctadecane), and DMA 18:1 (1,1-dimethoxy-(9Z)octadecene), in red blood cells is used to diagnosis plasmalogen synthesis disorders, including retinitis pigmentosa (RP), rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata and Zellweger disease spectrum. Schaefer et. al. (1995) found that dominant, recessive, and isolated forms of RP have higher ratios of DMA 16:0/C16:0, DMA 18:0/C18:0 and DMA 18:1n9/C18:1n9 compared to controls. For X-linked RP, only the DMA 18:1n9/C18:1n9 ratio was significantly higher than in controls.[PMID: 7595066] The ratio of DMA 18:0/C18:0 is reportedly lower in patients with In Zellweger syndrome.[PMID: 3760714]
[79344-8] Pyridoxal phosphate and pyridoxal and pyridoxine pattern [Interpretation] in Serum or Plasma Narrative -- **Component:** Pyridoxal phosphate & pyridoxal & pyridoxine pattern **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** PLP + PL + Vit B6 pattern SerPl-Imp  **Definition:** Narrative interpretation of results for pyridoxalphosphate, pyridoxal and pyridoxine levels in serum or plasma. [79346-3] Alpha-aminoadipic semialdehyde/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine -- **Component:** Alpha-aminoadipic semialdehyde/Creatinine **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Alpha-AASA/Creat Ur-sRto
[79347-1] Sulfate.total/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine -- **Component:** Sulfate.total/Creatinine **Property:** SRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** T sulfate/Creat Ur-sRto  **Definition:** Sulfur enters the body mainly as the amino acids, cysteine, and methionine. Food also contains inorganic and organic sulfates. The major end-product of sulfur metabolism in humans is inorganic sulfate, which is excreted in urine in the free form together with small amounts of its esters with aromatic compounds. [PMID: 4566470] However these two forms do not account for all the sulfur present in urine, and quantification of urinary total sulfur together with inorganic sulfate and ester sulfate is required for the evaluation of sulfur metabolism in humans. Men excrete significantly higher amounts of inorganic sulfate and total sulfur than woman, but this sex-related difference becomes insignificant if excretion values are expressed relative to creatinine. The trace element sulfur is essential to human life. [PMID: 7389089]  Sulfur is found in all cells. Skin and nail disease have been associated with an inadequate intake of sulfur containing amino acids. Psoriasis and rheumatic condition suffers often respond well to increased intakes of these amino acids.

[79378-6] Cause of death -- **Component:** Cause of death **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Cause of death  **Definition:** Official cause of death as coded from the death certificate in valid ICD-7, ICD-8, ICD-9, and ICD-10 codes. [79405-7] 1,4-Alpha glucan branching enzyme [Enzymatic activity/mass] in Fibroblast -- **Component:** 1,4-Alpha glucan branching enzyme **Property:** CCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** GLGB Fib-cCnt
[79406-5] IgM clearance/Albumin clearance [Ratio] in Serum and CSF -- **Component:** IgM clearance/Albumin clearance **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser+CSF **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** IgM/Alb clear Ser+CSF-Rto  **Definition:** CSF IgM, IgA, and IgG indexes are indicators of intrathecal immunoglobulin synthesis. The calculations incorporate the CSF-serum albumin quotient, an indicator of blood-brain-barrier (BBB) function, in order to differentiate whether the immunoglobulins found in the CSF were produced intrathecally or leaked across the BBB. These immunoglobulin indexes can be useful in the diagnosis of central nervous system infections, neoplasms and primary neurologic diseases such as multiple sclerosis. They also have the potential to monitor treatment efficacy in conditions such as systemic lupus erythematosus with CNS involvement, in which one study found that all three indexes decreased significantly along with CNS symptoms during successful treatment. The CSF IgM index is calculated as (CSF IgM/serum IgM)/(CSF albumin/serum albumin), which can also be represented as (CSF IgM x serum albumin)/(serum IgM x CSF albumin). [PMID: 3899036]

[79411-5] Creatine transport protein [Enzymatic activity/mass] in Fibroblast by Creatine uptake assay -- **Component:** Creatine transport protein **Property:** CCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Fibroblasts **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Creatine uptake  **Short name:** CrT protein Fib Creat uptake-cCnt  **Definition:** Creatine uptake via the creatine transport protein is determined by measuring the incorporation of creatine into fibroblasts. Decreased levels are associated with mutations in the SLC6A8 gene, which encodes the creatine transporter protein. The defect is clinically manifest in muscles cells, leading to muscle weakness. Creatine uptake is effected in many cell types, including fibroblasts, which are easy to collect. Therefore fibroblasts can be used as indicators cells for the creatine uptake defect.

[79418-0] EPCAM gene exons 8 and 9 deletion and duplication mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by MLPA -- **Component:** EPCAM gene exons 8 & 9 deletion+duplication **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** MLPA  **Short name:** EPCAM Exons 8 + 9 Del+Dup Bld/T MLPA  **Definition:** Multiple-ligation-probe amplification assays (MLPA) are performed to detect copy number variations (deletions and duplications) of exons 8 and 9 in the EPCAM (formerly known as TACSTD1) gene. [79419-8] PMS2 gene deletion+duplication and full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** PMS2 gene deletion+duplication & full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** PMS2 gene Del+Dup + Full Mut Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** The coding sequences of PMS2 are amplified by polymerase chain reaction and each PCR product (amplicon) then sequences bidirectionally using Sanger sequencing methodology. The multiple-ligation-probe amplification assay (MLPA) was performed to detect copy number variations (deletions and duplications) in the PMS2 gene. [79423-0] Cardiovascular disease 10Y risk [Likelihood] ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort by Goff 2013 -- **Component:** Cardiovascular disease 10Y risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort.Goff 2013  **Short name:** CVD 10Y risk ACC-AHA.Goff 2013  **Definition:** The quantitative estimate for a patient's 10-year risk for having an atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) based on the Pooled Cohort Equations as published in the 2013 ACC/AHA Guideline on the Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk.[PMID: 24222018] The 10-year ASCVD risk estimates are calculated using the patient's age, sex, race, total cholesterol, HDL cholesterol, systolic blood pressure, blood pressure lowering medication use, diabetes status, and smoking status. The equations were developed by the ACC/AHA Risk Assessment Work Group and derived from non-Hispanic white and African American populations from 40 to 79 years of age. For other ethnic groups, it is recommended using the equations for non-Hispanic whites, though these estimates may underestimate the risk for persons from some race/ethnic groups.
[79424-8] Serial sputum smears for diagnosing pulmonary tuberculosis panel - by Acid fast stain -- **Component:** Serial smears for diagnosing pulmonary tuberculosis panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Sputum **Scale:** - **Method:** Acid fast stain  **Short name:** Serial TB smears Pnl Spt Acid fast Stn  **Definition:** This panel is used to report the results of serial sputum smears used to diagnose pulmonary tuberculosis. Until 2007, the World Health Organization (WHO) recommended collecting three serial sputum specimens, including at least one first-morning specimen. In 2007, WHO reduced the number of specimens  from three to two, but only in places with an established external quality assurance (EQA) system for smear microscopy. In locations without such an EQA system, collection of three specimens is still recommended. [http://www.who.int/tb/laboratory/policy_diagnosis_pulmonary_tb/en/]  This panel originally only contained terms with the specific Method Acid fast stain.Ziehl-Neelsen but was updated in LOINC release 2.68 to accommodate the reporting of results for other acid fast stains.

[79529-4] Basic mobility score [AM-PAC] -- **Component:** Basic mobility score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** AM-PAC  **Short name:** Basic mobility score [AM-PAC]  **Definition:** The Boston University Activity Measure for Post-Acute Care (AM-PAC) instrument was developed as a functional outcomes measurement system that can be used across post-acute care settings. It assesses a set of functional activities in three separate areas - basic mobility, daily activity, and applied cognition - that most adults are likely to encounter during daily activities. Basic mobility includes basic movement and physical functioning activities, such as bending, walking, carrying, and climbing stairs. The electronic AM-PAC instrument results in a score and a standard error that represents the score's precision and is based on the number of questions answered. For example, answering more questions will result in higher precision as reflected by a lower standard error value.  [http://www.bu.edu/bostonroc/instruments/am-pac/]

[79563-3] Mucopolysaccharidosis type I newborn screening panel -- **Component:** Mucopolysaccharidosis type I newborn screening panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld.dot **Scale:** -  **Short name:** MPS I NBS Pnl DBS  **Definition:** The Mucopolysaccharidosis type I (MPS I) newborn screening panel is used to report the results of newborn screening for the diagnosis of MPS I, also known as Hurler syndrome, an inherited disorder in which the body is unable to produce an enzyme that is involved in the breakdown of large sugar molecules called glycosaminoglycans (GAGs). MPS I is caused by mutations in the IDUA gene and has an autosomal recessive pattern of inheritance.

[79570-8] MLH1+MSH2+MSH6+PMS2 gene deletion+duplication and full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** MLH1+MSH2+MSH6+PMS2 gene deletion+duplication & full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** MLH1+MSH2+MSH6+PMS2 gn Del+Dup+Ful M  **Definition:** Gene mutations, deletion and duplication analysis is performed to confirm a clinical diagnosis of Hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer (HNPCC). Genes known to cause HNPCC, including MLH1, MSH2, MSH6 and PMS2, may be amplified by PCR methods and sequenced. [79571-6] BCHE gene full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Sequencing -- **Component:** BCHE gene full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** BCHE gene Full Mut Anl Bld/T Seq
[79572-4] Folate and tryptophan and metabolites panel - Cerebral spinal fluid -- **Component:** Folate & tryptophan & metabolites panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Folate + Tryp + Metabs Pnl CSF  **Definition:** Testing for folate and its metabolites as well as tryptophan and its metabolites in CSF can be useful in distinguishing among various central nervous system disorders associated with folate deficiency. Low folate levels are associated with inborn errors of metabolism, including cerebral folate deficiency (CFD), a neurological syndrome that occurs around 2-4 months of age and is primarily associated with associated with low levels of 5-methyltetrahydrofolate (5-MTHF) in the CSF with normal folate levels in the plasma and red blood cells.[PMID: 19260931] Low folate levels are also associated with depression and dementia in elderly patients.[PMID: 11183038] Serotonin, which is formed from tryptophan, may be the link between folate and depression or dementia. Many patients with neuropsychiatric disorders and folate deficiency have also been found to have low levels of 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid (5-HIAA, a serotonin metabolite), and patients with 5,10-methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) deficiency also have decreased serotonin synthesis. [PMCID: 1810582]
[79573-2] Riboflavin and flavin mononucleotide and flavin adenine dinucleotide panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Riboflavin & flavin mononucleotide & flavin adenine dinucleotide panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Flavins Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** Riboflavin, flavin mononucleotide (FMN) and flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD) levels are measured for evaluation of riboflavin deficiency due to flavin synthesis/transport defects. Isolated riboflavin deficiency is rare and usually associated with other vitamin B complex deficiencies.[PMID: 18598588]
